The announcement represents a significant step toward autonomous scientific discovery, though the project raises questions about the role of human researchers.
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. OpenAI is throwing everything into building a fully automated researcher  OpenAI has a new grand challenge: building an AI researcher—a fully automated agent-based system capable of tackling large, complex problems by…

zh.title: 下载：OpenAI正在构建完全自动化的研究者，以及迷幻试验的盲点
zh.summary: OpenAI正在开发一个完全自动化的AI研究系统，能够通过基于代理的推理处理大型复杂问题。该公告代表了向自主科学发现迈出的重要一步，尽管该项目引发了关于人类研究者角色的问题。

fr.title: Le téléchargement : OpenAI construit un chercheur entièrement automatisé, et les angles morts des essais psychédéliques

L'annonce représente une étape importante vers la découverte scientifique autonome, bien que le projet soulève des questions sur le rôle des chercheurs humains.

Die Ankündigung stellt einen wichtigen Schritt zu autonomer wissenschaftlicher Entdeckung dar, wirft aber Fragen über die Rolle menschlicher Forscher auf.

The San Francisco firm has set its sights on building what it calls an AI researcher, a fully automated agent-based system that will be able to go off and tackle large, complex problems by itself. ​​OpenAI says that the new…

fr.title: OpenAI consacre tout à la création d'un chercheur entièrement automatisé

de.title: OpenAI konzentriert sich ganz auf die Entwicklung eines vollständig automatisierten Forschers
de.summary: OpenAI lenkt seine Forschungsbemühungen um, um einen KI-Forscher zu entwickeln—ein vollständig autonomes Agentensystem, das in der Lage ist, komplexe Probleme unabhängig zu bewältigen. Dies stellt eine bedeutende strategische Verschiebung hin zu autonomen Forschungskapazitäten dar.

es.summary: OpenAI está reorientando sus esfuerzos de investigación hacia la construcción de un investigador de IA, un sistema de agente completamente autónomo capaz de abordar de forma independiente problemas complejos. Esto representa un cambio estratégico significativo hacia capacidades de investigación autónoma.

title: Mind-altering substances are (still) falling short in clinical trials
summary: Psychedelic compounds like psilocybin continue to fall short of expectations in clinical trials despite potential for treating depression, PTSD, addiction, and obesity. The research highlights ongoing challenges in translating early promise into reliable therapeutic outcomes.
content: This week I want to look at where we are with psychedelics, the mind-altering substances that have somehow made the leap from counterculture to major focus of clinical research. Compounds like psilocybin—which is found in magic mushrooms—are being explored for all sorts of health applications, including treatments for depression, PTSD, addiction, and even obesity. Over…

zh.title: 改变心智的物质在临床试验中仍然表现不佳
zh.summary: 包括裸盖菇素在内的迷幻化合物在临床试验中继续未能达到预期，尽管它们对治疗抑郁症、创伤后应激障碍、成瘾和肥胖症具有潜力。这项研究强调了将早期承诺转化为可靠治疗效果的持续挑战。

fr.title: Les substances qui altèrent l'esprit ne répondent pas aux attentes dans les essais cliniques
fr.summary: Les composés psychédéliques comme la psilocybine continuent de ne pas répondre aux attentes dans les essais cliniques malgré leur potentiel pour traiter la dépression, le TSPT, la toxicomanie et l'obésité. La recherche souligne les défis continus de la traduction des promesses initiales en résultats thérapeutiques fiables.

de.title: Bewusstseinsverändernde Substanzen bleiben in klinischen Studien hinter den Erwartungen zurück
de.summary: Psychedelische Verbindungen wie Psilocybin bleiben in klinischen Studien weiterhin hinter den Erwartungen zurück, obwohl sie das Potenzial zur Behandlung von Depressionen, PTBS, Sucht und Fettleibigkeit haben. Die Forschung hebt die anhaltenden Herausforderungen bei der Umsetzung früher Versprechen in zuverlässige therapeutische Ergebnisse hervor.

es.title: Las sustancias que alteran la mente siguen siendo insuficientes en los ensayos clínicos
es.summary: Los compuestos psicodélicos como la psilocibina continúan siendo insuficientes en los ensayos clínicos a pesar del potencial para tratar la depresión, el TEPT, la adicción y la obesidad. La investigación destaca los desafíos continuos en la traducción de las promesas iniciales en resultados terapéuticos confiables.

title: DEAF: A Benchmark for Diagnostic Evaluation of Acoustic Faithfulness in Audio Language Models
summary: DEAF introduces a benchmark of 2,700+ conflict stimuli to evaluate whether Audio MLLMs genuinely process acoustic signals or rely on textual cues. The study reveals that while models are sensitive to acoustic variations, their predictions are predominantly driven by textual inputs, exposing a gap between high benchmark performance and true acoustic understanding.
content:
arXiv:2603.18048v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent Audio Multimodal Large Language Models (Audio MLLMs) demonstrate impressive performance on speech benchmarks, yet it remains unclear whether these models genuinely process acoustic signals or rely on text-based semantic inference. To systematically study this question, we introduce DEAF (Diagnostic Evaluation of Acoustic Faithfulness), a benchmark of over 2,700 conflict stimuli spanning three acoustic dimensions: emotional prosody, background sounds, and speaker identity. Then, we design a controlled multi-level evaluation framework that progressively increases textual influence, ranging from semantic conflicts in the content to misleading prompts and their combination, allowing us to disentangle content-driven bias from prompt-induced sycophancy. We further introduce diagnostic metrics to quantify model reliance on textual cues over acoustic signals. Our evaluation of seven Audio MLLMs reveals a consistent pattern of text dominance: models are sensitive to acoustic variations, yet predictions are predominantly driven by textual inputs, revealing a gap between high performance on standard speech benchmarks and genuine acoustic understanding.

zh.title: DEAF：音频语言模型声学忠实性的诊断评估基准
zh.summary: DEAF引入了一个包含2,700多个冲突刺激的基准，用于评估音频多模态模型是否真正处理声学信号或依赖文本线索。研究表明虽然模型对声学变化敏感，但预测主要由文本输入驱动，暴露了高基准性能与真正声学理解之间的差距。

fr.title: DEAF: Un benchmark pour l'évaluation diagnostique de la fidélité acoustique dans les modèles linguistiques audio
fr.summary: DEAF introduit un benchmark composé de plus de 2 700 stimuli conflictuels pour évaluer si les modèles multimodaux audio traitent réellement les signaux acoustiques ou s'appuient sur des indices textuels. L'étude révèle que bien que les modèles soient sensibles aux variations acoustiques, leurs prédictions sont principalement motivées par les entrées textuelles, exposant un écart entre les performances élevées du benchmark et la véritable compréhension acoustique.

de.title: DEAF: Ein Benchmark zur diagnostischen Bewertung der akustischen Authentizität in Audio-Sprachmodellen
de.summary: DEAF führt einen Benchmark mit über 2.700 Konfliktstimuli ein, um zu bewerten, ob Audio-Modelle tatsächlich akustische Signale verarbeiten oder sich auf Texthinweise verlassen. Die Studie zeigt, dass die Vorhersagen der Modelle zwar von akustischen Variationen beeinflusst werden, aber hauptsächlich durch Texteingaben getrieben werden, was eine Lücke zwischen hoher Benchmark-Leistung und echtem akustischen Verständnis offenbart.

es.title: DEAF: Un punto de referencia para la evaluación diagnóstica de la fidelidad acústica en modelos de lenguaje de audio
es.summary: DEAF introduce un punto de referencia de más de 2.700 estímulos conflictivos para evaluar si los modelos multimodales de audio procesan genuinamente señales acústicas o se basan en pistas textuales. El estudio revela que aunque los modelos son sensibles a las variaciones acústicas, sus predicciones están predominantemente impulsadas por entradas textuales, exponiendo una brecha entre el alto rendimiento del punto de referencia y la verdadera comprensión acústica.

title: Continually self-improving AI
summary: This thesis addresses three limitations of modern language models by proposing approaches for data-efficient knowledge acquisition through synthetic data, self-generated pretraining without human data, and AI-driven algorithm discovery. The work aims to enable continual self-improvement in AI systems beyond human-created constraints.
content:
arXiv:2603.18073v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Modern language model-based AI systems are remarkably powerful, yet their capabilities remain fundamentally capped by their human creators in three key ways. First, although a model's weights can be updated via fine-tuning, acquiring new knowledge from small, specialized corpora after pretraining remains highly data-inefficient. Second, the training of these systems relies heavily on finite, human-generated data from across history. Third, the pipelines used to train AI models are confined by the algorithms that human researchers can discover and explore. This thesis takes a small step toward overcoming these inherent limitations, presenting three chapters aimed at breaking these dependencies to create continually self-improving AI. First, to overcome this data-efficiency barrier in knowledge acquisition, we propose a synthetic data approach that diversifies and amplifies small corpora into rich knowledge representations, enabling a model to effectively update its parameters from limited source material. Second, to reduce reliance on human data, we show that given a fixed amount of such data, the model can self-generate synthetic data to bootstrap its fundamental pretraining capabilities without distillation from any off-the-shelf, instruction-tuned LM. Finally, to transcend human-engineered training paradigms, we demonstrate that by scaling search during test time over the space of algorithms, AI can search over a larger space of learning algorithm configurations than human researchers can explore manually.

zh.title: 持续自我改进的人工智能
zh.summary: 本论文通过提出数据高效知识获取、无需人类数据的自生成预训练和AI驱动的算法发现等方法，解决了现代语言模型的三个局限性。该工作旨在使AI系统能够在超越人类创建的约束条件下实现持续自我改进。

fr.title: L'IA s'améliorant continuellement elle-même
fr.summary: Cette thèse aborde trois limitations des modèles linguistiques modernes en proposant des approches pour l'acquisition de connaissances économes en données via des données synthétiques, l'apprentissage automatique généré par l'IA sans données humaines, et la découverte d'algorithmes pilotée par l'IA. Le travail vise à permettre l'auto-amélioration continue dans les systèmes d'IA au-delà des contraintes créées par l'homme.

de.title: Kontinuierlich selbstverbessernde KI
de.summary: Diese Dissertation behandelt drei Einschränkungen moderner Sprachmodelle, indem sie Ansätze für dateneffiziente Wissensakquisition durch synthetische Daten, selbstgeneriertes Vortraining ohne Humandaten und KI-gesteuerte Algorithmenfindung vorschlägt. Die Arbeit zielt darauf ab, kontinuierliche Selbstverbesserung in KI-Systemen jenseits menschlich geschaffener Einschränkungen zu ermöglichen.

es.title: IA que se mejora continuamente a sí misma
es.summary: Esta tesis aborda tres limitaciones de los modelos de lenguaje modernos al proponer enfoques para la adquisición de conocimientos eficiente en datos a través de datos sintéticos, preentrenamiento autogenerado sin datos humanos y descubrimiento de algoritmos impulsado por IA. El trabajo tiene como objetivo permitir la automejora continua en sistemas de IA más allá de las restricciones creadas por humanos.

title: Multi-Trait Subspace Steering to Reveal the Dark Side of Human-AI Interaction
summary: The paper develops MultiTraitsss framework to generate "dark models" that exhibit harmful behavioral patterns in human-AI interactions, addressing escalating risks like mental health crises from LLM guidance. Using controlled evaluations with these models, the authors propose protective measures to reduce harmful outcomes.
content:
arXiv:2603.18085v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent incidents have highlighted alarming cases where human-AI interactions led to negative psychological outcomes, including mental health crises and even user harm. As LLMs serve as sources of guidance, emotional support, and even informal therapy, these risks are poised to escalate. However, studying the mechanisms underlying harmful human-AI interactions presents significant methodological challenges, where organic harmful interactions typically develop over sustained engagement, requiring extensive conversational context that are difficult to simulate in controlled settings. To address this gap, we developed a Multi-Trait Subspace Steering (MultiTraitsss) framework that leverages established crisis-associated traits and novel subspace steering framework to generate Dark models that exhibits cumulative harmful behavioral patterns. Single-turn and multi-turn evaluations show that our dark models consistently produce harmful interaction and outcomes. Using our Dark models, we propose protective measure to reduce harmful outcomes in Human-AI interactions.

zh.title: 多特征子空间转向以揭示人工智能互动的黑暗面
zh.summary: 该论文开发了MultiTraits框架，用于生成展现人工智能互动中有害行为模式的"黑暗模型"，针对语言模型指导引发的心理健康危机等升级风险。作者利用这些模型的受控评估，提出了保护措施以减少有害结果。

fr.title: Pilotage multidimensionnel du sous-espace pour révéler le côté sombre de l'interaction humain-IA
fr.summary: L'article développe le cadre MultiTraits pour générer des "modèles sombres" qui présentent des schémas de comportement nuisible dans les interactions humain-IA, abordant les risques croissants comme les crises de santé mentale causées par les conseils des LLM. Utilisant des évaluations contrôlées avec ces modèles, les auteurs proposent des mesures de protection pour réduire les résultats nuisibles.

de.title: Multi-Merkmals-Unterraumsteuerung zur Enthüllung der dunklen Seite der Mensch-KI-Interaktion
de.summary: Das Papier entwickelt das MultiTraits-Framework, um "dunkle Modelle" zu generieren, die schädliche Verhaltensmuster in der Mensch-KI-Interaktion aufweisen und sich mit eskalierenden Risiken wie psychischen Gesundheitskrisen durch LLM-Beratung befassen. Mit kontrollierten Bewertungen mit diesen Modellen schlagen die Autoren Schutzmaßnahmen vor, um schädliche Ergebnisse zu reduzieren.

es.title: Dirección de subespacio de múltiples rasgos para revelar el lado oscuro de la interacción humano-IA
es.summary: El artículo desarrolla el marco MultiTraits para generar "modelos oscuros" que exhiben patrones de comportamiento dañino en interacciones humano-IA, abordando riesgos crecientes como crisis de salud mental por orientación de LLM. Utilizando evaluaciones controladas con estos modelos, los autores proponen medidas protectoras para reducir resultados dañinos.

title: Adaptive Domain Models: Bayesian Evolution, Warm Rotation, and Principled Training for Geometric and Neuromorphic AI
summary: This paper presents an alternative training architecture using posit arithmetic and geometric algebra to reduce training memory overhead to approximately twice inference footprint while preserving structural properties. It introduces Bayesian distillation for domain-specific adaptation and warm rotation for seamless model updates in live systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.18104v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Prevailing AI training infrastructure assumes reverse-mode automatic differentiation over IEEE-754 arithmetic. The memory overhead of training relative to inference, optimizer complexity, and structural degradation of geometric properties through training are consequences of this arithmetic substrate. This paper develops an alternative training architecture grounded in three prior results: the Dimensional Type System and Deterministic Memory Management framework [6], which establishes stack-eligible gradient allocation and exact quire accumulation as design-time verifiable properties; the Program Hypergraph [8], which establishes grade preservation through geometric algebra computations as a type-level invariant; and the b-posit 2026 standard [10], which makes posit arithmetic tractable across hardware targets conventionally considered inference-only. Their composition enables depth-independent training memory bounded to approximately twice the inference footprint, grade-preserving weight updates, and exact gradient accumulation, applicable uniformly to loss-function-optimized and spike-timing-dependent neuromorphic models. We introduce Bayesian distillation, a mechanism by which the latent prior structure of a general-purpose model is extracted through the ADM training regime, resolving the data-scarcity bootstrapping problem for domain-specific training. For deployment, we introduce warm rotation, an operational pattern in which an updated model transitions into an active inference pathway without service interruption, with structural correctness formalized through PHG certificates and signed version records. The result is a class of domain-specific AI systems that are smaller and more precise than general-purpose models, continuously adaptive, verifiably correct with respect to the physical structure of their domains, and initializable from existing models.

zh.title: 自适应域模型：贝叶斯进化、温启动和几何及神经形态AI的原理性训练
zh.summary: 本文提出了一种采用正数算术和几何代数的替代训练架构，将训练内存开销降低到约为推理占用空间的两倍，同时保留结构特性。它引入了贝叶斯蒸馏用于特定领域适配，以及温启动用于实时系统中的无缝模型更新。

fr.title: Modèles de domaine adaptatifs : Évolution bayésienne, rotation progressive et formation principiée pour l'IA géométrique et neuromorphique
fr.summary: Cet article présente une architecture d'entraînement alternative utilisant l'arithmétique positionnelle et l'algèbre géométrique pour réduire les frais généraux de mémoire d'entraînement à environ deux fois l'empreinte d'inférence tout en préservant les propriétés structurelles. Il introduit la distillation bayésienne pour l'adaptation spécifique au domaine et la rotation progressive pour les mises à jour de modèle transparentes dans les systèmes en direct.

de.title: Adaptive Domänenmodelle: Bayessche Evolution, Warm Rotation und principielle Schulung für geometrische und neuromorphe KI
de.summary: Dieses Papier präsentiert eine alternative Trainingsarchitektur mit Posit-Arithmetik und geometrischer Algebra, um den Trainings-Speicher-Overhead auf etwa das Doppelte des Inferenz-Fußabdrucks zu reduzieren und dabei strukturelle Eigenschaften zu bewahren. Es führt bayessche Destillation für domänenspezifische Anpassung und Warm Rotation für nahtlose Modellaktualisierungen in Live-Systemen ein.

es.title: Modelos de dominio adaptativo: evolución bayesiana, rotación cálida y entrenamiento principiado para IA geométrica y neuromorfa
es.summary: Este artículo presenta una arquitectura de entrenamiento alternativa que utiliza aritmética posit y álgebra geométrica para reducir la sobrecarga de memoria de entrenamiento a aproximadamente el doble de la huella de inferencia mientras se preservan las propiedades estructurales. Introduce destilación bayesiana para adaptación específica del dominio y rotación cálida para actualizaciones de modelo sin problemas en sistemas en vivo.

title: Don't Vibe Code, Do Skele-Code: Interactive No-Code Notebooks for Subject Matter Experts to Build Lower-Cost Agentic Workflows
summary: Skele-Code is a natural-language and graph-based interface enabling non-technical users to build AI agent workflows through interactive notebooks that convert to executable code. This approach reduces token costs compared to multi-agent systems while generating modular, shareable, and extensible workflows.
content:
arXiv:2603.18122v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Skele-Code is a natural-language and graph-based interface for building workflows with AI agents, designed especially for less or non-technical users. It supports incremental, interactive notebook-style development, and each step is converted to code with a required set of functions and behavior to enable incremental building of workflows. Agents are invoked only for code generation and error recovery, not orchestration or task execution. This agent-supported, but code-first approach to workflows, along with the context-engineering used in Skele-Code, can help reduce token costs compared to the multi-agent system approach to executing workflows. Skele-Code produces modular, easily extensible, and shareable workflows. The generated workflows can also be used as skills by agents, or as steps in other workflows.

zh.title: 不要Vibe编码，要Skele编码：为主题专家构建低成本智能体工作流的交互式无代码笔记本
zh.summary: Skele-Code是一种基于自然语言和图表的界面，使非技术用户能够通过转换为可执行代码的交互式笔记本来构建AI智能体工作流。与多智能体系统相比，这种方法降低了令牌成本，同时生成了模块化、可共享和可扩展的工作流。

fr.title: Ne pas viber le code, faire du Skele-Code : Des carnets interactifs sans code pour les experts en la matière pour construire des flux de travail d'agents rentables
fr.summary: Skele-Code est une interface basée sur le langage naturel et les graphiques permettant aux utilisateurs non techniques de créer des flux de travail d'agents IA via des carnets interactifs qui se convertissent en code exécutable. Cette approche réduit les coûts de jetons par rapport aux systèmes multi-agents tout en générant des flux de travail modulaires, partageables et extensibles.

de.title: Nicht Vibe Code, sondern Skele-Code: Interaktive No-Code-Notebooks für Fachexperten zum Erstellen kostengünstiger Agentic Workflows
de.summary: Skele-Code ist eine natürlichsprachige und grafikbasierte Schnittstelle, die Nicht-Techniker befähigt, AI-Agent-Workflows durch interaktive Notebooks zu erstellen, die in ausführbaren Code konvertiert werden. Dieser Ansatz reduziert Token-Kosten im Vergleich zu Multi-Agent-Systemen und erzeugt gleichzeitig modulare, teilbare und erweiterbare Workflows.

es.title: No hagas Vibe Code, haz Skele-Code: Cuadernos interactivos sin código para expertos en la materia construyan flujos de trabajo de agentes de bajo costo
es.summary: Skele-Code es una interfaz basada en lenguaje natural y gráficos que permite a usuarios no técnicos crear flujos de trabajo de agentes de IA a través de cuadernos interactivos que se convierten en código ejecutable. Este enfoque reduce los costos de tokens en comparación con los sistemas multiagente, mientras genera flujos de trabajo modulares, compartibles y extensibles.

title: Efficient Dense Crowd Trajectory Prediction Via Dynamic Clustering
summary: The paper proposes a cluster-based approach that groups individuals by shared attributes to accelerate dense crowd trajectory prediction while reducing memory usage. The method maintains prediction accuracy while enabling faster processing on challenging crowd scenarios with noisy tracking data.
content:
arXiv:2603.18166v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Crowd trajectory prediction plays a crucial role in public safety and management, where it can help prevent disasters such as stampedes. Recent works address the problem by predicting individual trajectories and considering surrounding objects based on manually annotated data. However, these approaches tend to overlook dense crowd scenarios, where the challenges of automation become more pronounced due to the massiveness, noisiness, and inaccuracy of the tracking outputs, resulting in high computational costs. To address these challenges, we propose and extensively evaluate a novel cluster-based approach that groups individuals based on similar attributes over time, enabling faster execution through accurate group summarisation. Our plug-and-play method can be combined with existing trajectory predictors by using our output centroid in place of their pedestrian input. We evaluate our proposed method on several challenging dense crowd scenes. We demonstrated that our approach leads to faster processing and lower memory usage when compared with state-of-the-art methods, while maintaining the accuracy

zh.title: 通过动态聚类的高效密集人群轨迹预测
zh.summary: 本文提出了一种基于聚类的方法，通过共享属性对个体进行分组，以加快密集人群轨迹预测，同时减少内存使用。该方法在保持预测准确性的同时，能够在具有噪声跟踪数据的复杂人群场景中实现更快的处理。

fr.title: Prédiction efficace de trajectoire de foule dense via clustering dynamique
fr.summary: L'article propose une approche basée sur le clustering qui regroupe les individus par attributs partagés pour accélérer la prédiction de trajectoire de foule dense tout en réduisant l'utilisation de la mémoire. La méthode maintient la précision des prédictions tout en permettant un traitement plus rapide sur des scénarios de foule difficiles avec des données de suivi bruitées.

de.title: Effiziente dichte Personenmassen-Bahnvorhersage durch dynamisches Clustering
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt einen Cluster-basierten Ansatz vor, der Einzelpersonen nach gemeinsamen Attributen gruppiert, um die Vorhersage dichter Personenmassen-Bahnen zu beschleunigen und gleichzeitig die Speichernutzung zu reduzieren. Die Methode erhält die Vorhersagegenauigkeit bei, während sie eine schnellere Verarbeitung bei anspruchsvollen Menschengedränge-Szenarien mit verrauschten Verfolgungsdaten ermöglicht.

es.title: Predicción eficiente de trayectoria de multitudes densas mediante agrupamiento dinámico
es.summary: El documento propone un enfoque basado en agrupamiento que agrupa individuos por atributos compartidos para acelerar la predicción de trayectoria de multitudes densas mientras reduce el uso de memoria. El método mantiene la precisión de la predicción mientras permite un procesamiento más rápido en escenarios de multitudes desafiantes con datos de seguimiento ruidosos.

title: TeachingCoach: A Fine-Tuned Scaffolding Chatbot for Instructional Guidance to Instructors
summary: TeachingCoach is a pedagogically-grounded chatbot that provides real-time instructional guidance to higher education instructors through synthetic data-driven fine-tuning. Expert evaluations show it produces clearer, more reflective guidance than GPT-4o mini, offering a scalable approach to instructor professional development.
content:
arXiv:2603.18189v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Higher education instructors often lack timely and pedagogically grounded support, as scalable instructional guidance remains limited and existing tools rely on generic chatbot advice or non-scalable teaching center human-human consultations. We present TeachingCoach, a pedagogically grounded chatbot designed to support instructor professional development through real-time, conversational guidance. TeachingCoach is built on a data-centric pipeline that extracts pedagogical rules from educational resources and uses synthetic dialogue generation to fine-tune a specialized language model that guides instructors through problem identification, diagnosis, and strategy development. Expert evaluations show TeachingCoach produces clearer, more reflective, and more responsive guidance than a GPT-4o mini baseline, while a user study with higher education instructors highlights trade-offs between conversational depth and interaction efficiency. Together, these results demonstrate that pedagogically grounded, synthetic data driven chatbots can improve instructional support and offer a scalable design approach for future instructional chatbot systems.

zh.title: TeachingCoach：为讲师提供教学指导的微调脚手架聊天机器人
zh.summary: TeachingCoach是一个以教育学为基础的聊天机器人，通过合成数据驱动的微调为高等教育讲师提供实时教学指导。专家评估显示它比GPT-4o mini产生更清晰、更具反思性的指导，为讲师专业发展提供了一种可扩展的方法。

fr.title: TeachingCoach : Un chatbot d'échafaudage ajusté pour les conseils pédagogiques aux instructeurs
fr.summary: TeachingCoach est un chatbot fondé pédagogiquement qui fournit des conseils pédagogiques en temps réel aux instructeurs de l'enseignement supérieur grâce à un ajustement fin piloté par les données synthétiques. Les évaluations d'experts montrent qu'il produit des conseils plus clairs et plus réfléchis que GPT-4o mini, offrant une approche évolutive au développement professionnel des instructeurs.

de.title: TeachingCoach: Ein fein abgestimmter Scaffolding-Chatbot für Unterrichtsanleitung für Dozenten
de.summary: TeachingCoach ist ein pädagogisch fundierter Chatbot, der Hochschuldozenten durch synthetische, datengesteuerte Fine-Tuning-Anweisung in Echtzeit bereitstellt. Expertenauswertungen zeigen, dass er klarer und reflektierter Anleitung als GPT-4o mini bietet und einen skalierbaren Ansatz für die berufliche Entwicklung von Dozenten darstellt.

es.title: TeachingCoach: Un chatbot de andamiaje ajustado para orientación instruccional a instructores
es.summary: TeachingCoach es un chatbot fundamentado pedagógicamente que proporciona orientación instruccional en tiempo real a instructores de educación superior a través de ajuste fino impulsado por datos sintéticos. Las evaluaciones de expertos muestran que produce orientación más clara y reflexiva que GPT-4o mini, ofreciendo un enfoque escalable para el desarrollo profesional de instructores.

title: Access Controlled Website Interaction for Agentic AI with Delegated Critical Tasks
summary: The paper proposes a website design with fine-grained access control mechanisms enabling AI agents to safely delegate critical tasks on users' behalf. It includes modifications to authorization protocols specifically tailored for agentic AI interactions with delegated responsibilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.18197v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent studies reveal gaps in delegating critical tasks to agentic AI that accesses websites on the user's behalf, primarily due to limited access control mechanisms on websites designed for agentic AI. In response, we propose a design of website-based interaction for AI agents with fine-grained access control for delegated critical tasks. Our approach encompasses a website design and implementation, as well as modifications to the access grant protocols in an open-source authorization service to tailor it to agentic AI, with delegated critical tasks on the website. The evaluation of our approach demonstrates the capabilities of our access-controlled website used by AI agents.

zh.title: 具有委托关键任务的代理AI的访问控制网站交互
zh.summary: 该论文提出了具有细粒度访问控制机制的网站设计，使AI代理能够安全地代表用户委托关键任务。它包括针对具有委托责任的代理AI交互专门定制的授权协议修改。

fr.title: Interaction de site Web contrôlée par accès pour l'IA agentive avec tâches critiques déléguées
fr.summary: L'article propose une conception de site Web avec des mécanismes de contrôle d'accès granulaires permettant aux agents d'IA de déléguer en toute sécurité des tâches critiques au nom des utilisateurs. Il comprend des modifications des protocoles d'autorisation spécifiquement adaptées aux interactions d'IA agentive avec des responsabilités déléguées.

de.title: Zugangsgesteuerte Website-Interaktion für agentische KI mit delegierten kritischen Aufgaben
de.summary: Das Dokument schlägt ein Website-Design mit feinkörnigen Zugriffskontrollmechanismen vor, die es KI-Agenten ermöglichen, kritische Aufgaben sicher im Auftrag von Benutzern zu delegieren. Es umfasst Änderungen an Autorisierungsprotokollen, die speziell für agentische KI-Interaktionen mit delegierten Verantwortlichkeiten maßgeschneidert sind.

es.title: Interacción de sitio web controlada por acceso para IA agentiva con tareas críticas delegadas
es.summary: El documento propone un diseño de sitio web con mecanismos de control de acceso granular que permitan a los agentes de IA delegar de forma segura tareas críticas en nombre de los usuarios. Incluye modificaciones de los protocolos de autorización diseñadas específicamente para interacciones de IA agentiva con responsabilidades delegadas.

title: A Computationally Efficient Learning of Artificial Intelligence System Reliability Considering Error Propagation
summary: This paper develops a reliability modeling framework using autonomous vehicle simulation and composite likelihood EM algorithm to quantify error propagation across AI system stages. The computationally efficient approach addresses data scarcity and complex interdependencies in modeling real-world AI system reliability.
content:
arXiv:2603.18201v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly prominent in emerging smart cities, yet their reliability remains a critical concern. These systems typically operate through a sequence of interconnected functional stages, where upstream errors may propagate to downstream stages, ultimately affecting overall system reliability. Quantifying such error propagation is essential for accurate modeling of AI system reliability. However, this task is challenging due to: i) data availability: real-world AI system reliability data are often scarce and constrained by privacy concerns; ii) model validity: recurring error events across sequential stages are interdependent, violating the independence assumptions of statistical inference; and iii) computational complexity: AI systems process large volumes of high-speed data, resulting in frequent and complex recurrent error events that are difficult to track and analyze. To address these challenges, this paper leverages a physics-based autonomous vehicle simulation platform with a justifiable error injector to generate high-quality data for AI system reliability analysis. Building on this data, a new reliability modeling framework is developed to explicitly characterize error propagation across stages. Model parameters are estimated using a computationally efficient, theoretically guaranteed composite likelihood expectation - maximization algorithm. Its application to the reliability modeling for autonomous vehicle perception systems demonstrates its predictive accuracy and computational efficiency.

zh.title: 考虑误差传播的人工智能系统可靠性的计算高效学习
zh.summary: 本论文开发了一个使用自主车辆仿真和复合似然EM算法的可靠性建模框架，用于量化AI系统各阶段的误差传播。这种计算高效的方法解决了实际AI系统可靠性建模中的数据稀缺性和复杂相互依赖性问题。

fr.title: Apprentissage efficace sur le plan informatique de la fiabilité des systèmes d'intelligence artificielle en tenant compte de la propagation des erreurs
fr.summary: Cet article développe un cadre de modélisation de la fiabilité utilisant la simulation de véhicules autonomes et l'algorithme EM de vraisemblance composite pour quantifier la propagation des erreurs dans les étapes du système d'IA. L'approche informatiquement efficace aborde la rareté des données et les interdépendances complexes dans la modélisation de la fiabilité des systèmes d'IA du monde réel.

de.title: Recheneffizientes Lernen der Zuverlässigkeit von Künstliche-Intelligenz-Systemen unter Berücksichtigung der Fehlerausbreitung
de.summary: Dieses Dokument entwickelt ein Zuverlässigkeitsmodellierungsrahmen unter Verwendung von Simulation autonomer Fahrzeuge und des zusammengesetzten Likelihood-EM-Algorithmus, um die Fehlerausbreitung über KI-Systemstufen zu quantifizieren. Der rechnerisch effiziente Ansatz befasst sich mit Datenmangel und komplexen gegenseitigen Abhängigkeiten bei der Modellierung der Zuverlässigkeit realer KI-Systeme.

es.title: Aprendizaje computacionalmente eficiente de la confiabilidad del sistema de inteligencia artificial considerando propagación de errores
es.summary: Este documento desarrolla un marco de modelado de confiabilidad utilizando simulación de vehículos autónomos y algoritmo EM de probabilidad compuesta para cuantificar la propagación de errores en etapas del sistema de IA. El enfoque computacionalmente eficiente aborda la escasez de datos y las interdependencias complejas en el modelado de confiabilidad del sistema de IA en el mundo real.

title: Retrieval-Augmented LLM Agents: Learning to Learn from Experience
summary: The paper combines supervised fine-tuning with experience retrieval to train LLM agents that better leverage retrieved trajectories for generalization to unseen tasks. Systematic analysis of storage and retrieval strategies shows this integrated approach significantly outperforms either method alone.
content:
arXiv:2603.18272v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While large language models (LLMs) have advanced the development of general-purpose agents, achieving robust generalization to unseen tasks remains a significant challenge. Current approaches typically rely on either fine-tuning or training-free memory-augmented generation using retrieved experience; yet both have limitations: fine-tuning often fails to extrapolate to new tasks, while experience retrieval often underperforms compared to supervised baselines. In this work, we propose to combine these approaches and systematically study how to train retrieval-augmented LLM agents to effectively leverage retrieved trajectories in-context. First, we establish a robust supervised fine-tuning (SFT) recipe using LoRA that outperforms several state-of-the-art agent training pipelines. Second, we provide a detailed analysis of key design choices for experience retrieval, identifying optimal strategies for storage, querying, and trajectory selection. Finally, we propose a pipeline that integrates experience retrieval into the fine-tuning process. Our results demonstrate that this combined approach significantly improves generalization to unseen tasks, providing a scalable and effective framework for building agents that learn to learn from experience.

zh.title: 检索增强型大语言模型代理：从经验中学习
zh.summary: 该论文结合监督微调和经验检索来训练大语言模型代理，使其能够更好地利用检索的轨迹来泛化到未见过的任务。对存储和检索策略的系统分析表明，这种集成方法明显优于任何一种方法单独使用。

fr.title: Agents LLM augmentés par récupération : apprendre à apprendre de l'expérience
fr.summary: L'article combine l'ajustement fin supervisé avec la récupération d'expérience pour former des agents LLM qui exploitent mieux les trajectoires récupérées pour généraliser vers des tâches jamais vues. L'analyse systématique des stratégies de stockage et de récupération montre que cette approche intégrée surpasse nettement l'une ou l'autre méthode seule.

de.title: Abruf-verstärkte LLM-Agenten: Lernen, aus Erfahrung zu lernen
de.summary: Das Papier kombiniert überwachtes Feintuning mit Erfahrungsabruf, um LLM-Agenten zu trainieren, die abgerufene Trajektorien besser nutzen, um sich auf unbekannte Aufgaben zu verallgemeinern. Die systematische Analyse von Speicher- und Abrufstrategien zeigt, dass dieser integrierte Ansatz beide Methoden allein deutlich übertrifft.

es.title: Agentes LLM aumentados por recuperación: Aprender a aprender de la experiencia
es.summary: El artículo combina ajuste fino supervisado con recuperación de experiencia para entrenar agentes LLM que mejor aprovechen trayectorias recuperadas para generalización a tareas nunca vistas. El análisis sistemático de estrategias de almacenamiento y recuperación muestra que este enfoque integrado supera significativamente cualquiera de los métodos por sí solo.

title: EDM-ARS: A Domain-Specific Multi-Agent System for Automated Educational Data Mining Research
summary: EDM-ARS is a multi-agent research pipeline orchestrating five specialized LLM agents through state-machine coordination to automate educational data mining research end-to-end. It produces complete LaTeX manuscripts with validated analyses, real citations, and automated methodological review for predictive modeling tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.18273v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In this technical report, we present the Educational Data Mining Automated Research System (EDM-ARS), a domain-specific multi-agent pipeline that automates end-to-end educational data mining (EDM) research. We conceptualize EDM-ARS as a general framework for domain-aware automated research pipelines, where educational expertise is embedded into each stage of the research lifecycle. As a first instantiation of this framework, we focus on predictive modeling tasks. Within this scope, EDM-ARS orchestrates five specialized LLM-powered agents (ProblemFormulator, DataEngineer, Analyst, Critic, and Writer) through a state-machine coordinator that supports revision loops, checkpoint-based recovery, and sandboxed code execution. Given a research prompt and a dataset, EDM-ARS produces a complete LaTeX manuscript with real Semantic Scholar citations, validated machine learning analyses, and automated methodological peer review. We also provide a detailed description of the system architecture, the three-tier data registry design that encodes educational domain expertise, the specification of each agent, the inter-agent communication protocol, and mechanisms for error-handling and self-correction. Finally, we discuss current limitations, including single-dataset scope and formulaic paper output, and outline a phased roadmap toward causal inference, transfer learning, psychometric, and multi-dataset generalization. EDM-ARS is released as an open-source project to support the educational research community.

zh.title: EDM-ARS：用于自动化教育数据挖掘研究的领域特定多代理系统
zh.summary: EDM-ARS是一个多代理研究管道，通过状态机协调编排五个专业化的大语言模型代理，以端到端地自动化教育数据挖掘研究。它生成完整的LaTeX手稿，包含验证的分析、真实引用和对预测建模任务的自动方法论审查。

fr.title: EDM-ARS : Un système multi-agent spécifique au domaine pour la recherche automatisée en extraction de données éducatives
fr.summary: EDM-ARS est un pipeline de recherche multi-agent orchestrant cinq agents LLM spécialisés via coordination par machine d'état pour automatiser complètement la recherche en extraction de données éducatives. Il produit des manuscrits LaTeX complets avec des analyses validées, des citations réelles et un examen méthodologique automatisé pour les tâches de modélisation prédictive.

de.title: EDM-ARS: Ein domänenspezifisches Multi-Agent-System für automatisierte Forschung zum Data Mining von Bildungsdaten
de.summary: EDM-ARS ist eine Multi-Agent-Forschungs-Pipeline, die fünf spezialisierte LLM-Agenten durch State-Machine-Koordination orchestriert, um die Forschung zum Bildungsdaten-Mining end-to-end zu automatisieren. Es produziert vollständige LaTeX-Manuskripte mit validierten Analysen, echten Zitaten und automatisierter methodischer Überprüfung für Aufgaben der prädiktiven Modellierung.

es.title: EDM-ARS: Un sistema multi-agente específico del dominio para investigación automatizada de minería de datos educativos
es.summary: EDM-ARS es un pipeline de investigación multi-agente que orquesta cinco agentes LLM especializados mediante coordinación de máquina de estados para automatizar completamente la investigación de minería de datos educativos. Produce manuscritos LaTeX completos con análisis validados, citas reales y revisión metodológica automatizada para tareas de modelado predictivo.

title: CORE: Robust Out-of-Distribution Detection via Confidence and Orthogonal Residual Scoring
summary: CORE decomposes penultimate features into orthogonal subspaces to independently score confidence and membership signals, overcoming limitations where these signals are entangled in existing methods. The approach achieves state-of-the-art out-of-distribution detection performance across multiple architectures with negligible computational overhead.
content:
arXiv:2603.18290v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Out-of-distribution (OOD) detection is essential for deploying deep learning models reliably, yet no single method performs consistently across architectures and datasets -- a scorer that leads on one benchmark often falters on another. We attribute this inconsistency to a shared structural limitation: logit-based methods see only the classifier's confidence signal, while feature-based methods attempt to measure membership in the training distribution but do so in the full feature space where confidence and membership are entangled, inheriting architecture-sensitive failure modes. We observe that penultimate features naturally decompose into two orthogonal subspaces: a classifier-aligned component encoding confidence, and a residual the classifier discards. We discover that this residual carries a class-specific directional signature for in-distribution data -- a membership signal invisible to logit-based methods and entangled with noise in feature-based methods. We propose CORE (COnfidence + REsidual), which disentangles the two signals by scoring each subspace independently and combines them via normalized summation. Because the two signals are orthogonal by construction, their failure modes are approximately independent, producing robust detection where either view alone is unreliable. CORE achieves competitive or state-of-the-art performance across five architectures and five benchmark configurations, ranking first in three of five settings and achieving the highest grand average AUROC with negligible computational overhead.

zh.title: CORE：通过置信度和正交残差评分的鲁棒分布外检测
zh.summary: CORE将倒数第二层特征分解为正交子空间，以独立地评分置信度和成员信号，克服了这些信号在现有方法中纠缠的局限性。该方法在多种架构中实现了最先进的分布外检测性能，计算开销可忽略不计。

fr.title: CORE : Détection robuste hors distribution via notation de confiance et résidu orthogonal
fr.summary: CORE décompose les caractéristiques pénultièmes en sous-espaces orthogonaux pour noter indépendamment les signaux de confiance et d'appartenance, surmontant les limitations où ces signaux sont enchevêtrés dans les méthodes existantes. L'approche atteint les performances de détection hors distribution de pointe sur plusieurs architectures avec une surcharge de calcul négligeable.

de.title: CORE: Robuste Out-of-Distribution-Erkennung durch Konfidenz- und orthogonale Residualbewertung
de.summary: CORE zerlegt vorletzte Features in orthogonale Unterräume, um Konfidenz- und Zugehörigkeitssignale unabhängig zu bewerten und überwindet Einschränkungen, bei denen diese Signale in bestehenden Methoden verflochten sind. Der Ansatz erreitet hochmoderne Out-of-Distribution-Erkennungsleistung über mehrere Architekturen hinweg mit vernachlässigbarem Rechenaufwand.

es.title: CORE: Detección robusta fuera de distribución mediante puntuación de confianza y residuos ortogonales
es.summary: CORE descompone características penúltimas en subespacios ortogonales para puntuar independientemente señales de confianza y pertenencia, superando limitaciones donde estas señales se entrelazan en métodos existentes. El enfoque logra rendimiento de detección fuera de distribución de última generación en múltiples arquitecturas con gastos computacionales negligibles.

title: The Validity Gap in Health AI Evaluation: A Cross-Sectional Analysis of Benchmark Composition
summary: This analysis of 18,707 health queries across six benchmarks reveals significant misalignment between health AI evaluation and real-world clinical needs, with insufficient coverage of complex diagnostics, safety-critical scenarios, and vulnerable populations. The findings call for standardized query profiling analogous to clinical trial reporting standards.
content:
arXiv:2603.18294v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Background: Clinical trials rely on transparent inclusion criteria to ensure generalizability. In contrast, benchmarks validating health-related large language models (LLMs) rarely characterize the "patient" or "query" populations they contain. Without defined composition, aggregate performance metrics may misrepresent model readiness for clinical use.
  Methods: We analyzed 18,707 consumer health queries across six public benchmarks using LLMs as automated coding instruments to apply a standardized 16-field taxonomy profiling context, topic, and intent.
  Results: We identified a structural "validity gap." While benchmarks have evolved from static retrieval to interactive dialogue, clinical composition remains misaligned with real-world needs. Although 42% of the corpus referenced objective data, this was polarized toward wellness-focused wearable signals (17.7%); complex diagnostic inputs remained rare, including laboratory values (5.2%), imaging (3.8%), and raw medical records (0.6%). Safety-critical scenarios were effectively absent: suicide/self-harm queries comprised <0.7% of the corpus and chronic disease management only 5.5%. Benchmarks also neglected vulnerable populations (pediatrics/older adults <11%) and global health needs.
  Conclusions: Evaluation benchmarks remain misaligned with real-world clinical needs, lacking raw clinical artifacts, adequate representation of vulnerable populations, and longitudinal chronic care scenarios. The field must adopt standardized query profiling--analogous to clinical trial reporting--to align evaluation with the full complexity of clinical practice.

zh.title: 健康AI评估中的有效性差距：基准组成的横断面分析
zh.summary: 对跨六个基准的18,707个健康查询的分析揭示了健康AI评估与真实临床需求之间的显著错位，对复杂诊断、安全关键场景和弱势人群的覆盖不足。研究结果呼吁采用类似于临床试验报告标准的标准化查询分析。

fr.title: L'écart de validité dans l'évaluation de l'IA pour la santé : une analyse transversale de la composition des benchmarks
fr.summary: Cette analyse de 18 707 requêtes sanitaires à travers six benchmarks révèle un désalignement significatif entre l'évaluation de l'IA sanitaire et les besoins cliniques réels, avec une couverture insuffisante des diagnostics complexes, des scénarios critiques pour la sécurité et des populations vulnérables. Les résultats appellent à un profilage standardisé des requêtes analogue aux normes de déclaration des essais cliniques.

de.title: Die Validitätslücke in der Bewertung von Gesundheits-KI: Eine Querschnittsanalyse der Benchmark-Zusammensetzung
de.summary: Diese Analyse von 18.707 Gesundheitsabfragen über sechs Benchmarks offenbart erhebliche Misalignment zwischen der Bewertung von Gesundheits-KI und realen klinischen Bedürfnissen, mit unzureichender Abdeckung komplexer Diagnostik, sicherheitskritischer Szenarien und gefährdeter Bevölkerungsgruppen. Die Ergebnisse fordern eine standardisierte Abfrageprofilierung analog zu klinischen Versuchsberichtstandards.

es.title: La brecha de validez en la evaluación de IA para la salud: un análisis transversal de la composición de puntos de referencia
es.summary: Este análisis de 18.707 consultas sanitarias en seis puntos de referencia revela un desalineamiento significativo entre la evaluación de IA para la salud y las necesidades clínicas del mundo real, con cobertura insuficiente de diagnósticos complejos, escenarios críticos para la seguridad y poblaciones vulnerables. Los hallazgos solicitan un perfilado de consultas estandarizado análogo a los estándares de informe de ensayos clínicos.

title: Consumer-to-Clinical Language Shifts in Ambient AI Draft Notes and Clinician-Finalized Documentation: A Multi-level Analysis
summary: The study quantifies clinician edits of AI-generated draft clinical notes, finding significant shifts from consumer-oriented to standardized clinical terminology across all note sections. Editing intensity varied by clinician and section, with assessment and plan accounting for 59% of terminology transformations.
content:
arXiv:2603.18327v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Ambient AI generates draft clinical notes from patient-clinician conversations, often using lay or consumer-oriented phrasing to support patient understanding instead of standardized clinical terminology. How clinicians revise these drafts for professional documentation conventions remains unclear. We quantified clinician editing for consumer-to- clinical normalization using a dictionary-confirmed transformation framework. We analyzed 71,173 AI-draft and finalized-note section pairs from 34,726 encounters. Confirmed transformations were defined as replacing a consumer expression with its dictionary-mapped clinical equivalent in the same section. Editing significantly reduced terminology density across all sections (p < 0.001). The Assessment and Plan accounted for the largest transformation volume (59.3%). Our analysis identified 7,576 transformation events across 4,114 note sections (5.8%), representing 1.2% consumer-term deletions. Transformation intensity varied across individual clinicians (p < 0.001). Overall, clinician post-editing demonstrates consistent shifts from conversational phrasing toward standardized, section- appropriate clinical terminology, supporting section-aware ambient AI design.

zh.title: 环境AI草稿记录和临床医生最终文档中的消费者到临床语言转变：多层次分析
zh.summary: 该研究量化了临床医生对AI生成的草稿临床记录的编辑，发现所有记录部分中从消费者导向到标准化临床术语的重大转变。编辑强度因临床医生和部分而异，评估和计划占术语转变的59%。

fr.title: Changements de langage du consommateur au clinique dans les brouillons d'IA ambiante et la documentation finalisée par le clinicien : une analyse multi-niveaux
fr.summary: L'étude quantifie les modifications apportées par les cliniciens aux brouillons de notes cliniques générées par l'IA, mettant en évidence des changements significatifs de la terminologie orientée vers les consommateurs à la terminologie clinique standardisée dans toutes les sections de notes. L'intensité de la modification variait selon le clinicien et la section, l'évaluation et le plan représentant 59% des transformations terminologiques.

de.title: Verbraucher-zu-Klinische-Sprachverschiebungen in Ambient-AI-Entwurfsnoten und von Klinikern finalisierter Dokumentation: Eine mehrstufige Analyse
de.summary: Die Studie quantifiziert die Bearbeitungen von Klinikern an KI-generierten Klinischentwurfsnoten und findet signifikante Verschiebungen von verbraucherorientierter zu standardisierter klinischer Terminologie über alle Notizabschnitte hinweg. Die Bearbeitungsintensität variierte nach Kliniker und Abschnitt, wobei Bewertung und Plan für 59% der Terminologietransformationen verantwortlich waren.

es.title: Cambios de lenguaje de consumidor a clínico en notas de borradores de IA ambiental y documentación finalizada por clínico: un análisis multinivel
es.summary: El estudio cuantifica las ediciones de clínicos de notas clínicas de borrador generadas por IA, encontrando cambios significativos de terminología orientada al consumidor a terminología clínica estandarizada en todas las secciones de notas. La intensidad de edición varió según el clínico y la sección, con evaluación y plan representando el 59% de las transformaciones terminológicas.

title: FaithSteer-BENCH: A Deployment-Aligned Stress-Testing Benchmark for Inference-Time Steering
summary: FaithSteer-BENCH evaluates inference-time steering methods under realistic deployment constraints and reveals systematic failure modes including illusory controllability and brittleness under perturbations. The benchmark demonstrates that existing steering methods lack reliability for practical deployment and proposes clearer criteria for future method design.
content:
arXiv:2603.18329v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Inference-time steering is widely regarded as a lightweight and parameter-free mechanism for controlling large language model (LLM) behavior, and prior work has often suggested that simple activation-level interventions can reliably induce targeted behavioral changes. However, such conclusions are typically drawn under relatively relaxed evaluation settings that overlook deployment constraints, capability trade-offs, and real-world robustness. We therefore introduce \textbf{FaithSteer-BENCH}, a stress-testing benchmark that evaluates steering methods at a fixed deployment-style operating point through three gate-wise criteria: controllability, utility preservation, and robustness. Across multiple models and representative steering approaches, we uncover several systematic failure modes that are largely obscured under standard evaluation, including illusory controllability, measurable cognitive tax on unrelated capabilities, and substantial brittleness under mild instruction-level perturbations, role prompts, encoding transformations, and data scarcity. Gate-wise benchmark results show that existing methods do not necessarily provide reliable controllability in deployment-oriented practical settings. In addition, mechanism-level diagnostics indicate that many steering methods induce prompt-conditional alignment rather than stable latent directional shifts, further explaining their fragility under stress. FaithSteer-BENCH therefore provides a unified benchmark and a clearer analytical lens for future method design, reliability evaluation, and deployment-oriented research in steering.

zh.title: FaithSteer-BENCH：一个面向部署的推理时间引导压力测试基准
zh.summary: FaithSteer-BENCH在现实的部署约束下评估推理时间引导方法，揭示了系统性故障模式，包括虚幻可控性和在扰动下的脆性。该基准表明现有的引导方法缺乏实际部署的可靠性，并为未来方法设计提出了更清晰的标准。

fr.title: FaithSteer-BENCH : Un benchmark de test de stress aligné sur le déploiement pour l'orientation au moment de l'inférence
fr.summary: FaithSteer-BENCH évalue les méthodes d'orientation au moment de l'inférence sous des contraintes de déploiement réalistes et révèle des modes de défaillance systématiques, notamment la contrôlabilité illusoire et la fragilité sous les perturbations. L'évaluation de référence montre que les méthodes d'orientation existantes manquent de fiabilité pour le déploiement pratique et propose des critères plus clairs pour la conception future des méthodes.

de.title: FaithSteer-BENCH: Ein bereitstellungsgerichteter Stresstest-Benchmark für Inferenz-Zeit-Lenkung
de.summary: FaithSteer-BENCH bewertet Inferenz-Zeit-Lenkmethoden unter realistischen Bereitstellungsbeschränkungen und offenbart systematische Fehlermodi, einschließlich illusorischer Kontrollierbarkeit und Sprödigkeit unter Störungen. Die Benchmark zeigt, dass bestehende Lenkmethoden für praktische Bereitstellung unzuverlässig sind, und schlägt klarere Kriterien für zukünftiges Methodendesign vor.

es.title: FaithSteer-BENCH: Un benchmark de prueba de estrés alineado con la implementación para la dirección en tiempo de inferencia
es.summary: FaithSteer-BENCH evalúa métodos de dirección en tiempo de inferencia bajo restricciones de implementación realistas y revela modos de fallo sistemáticos, incluyendo controlabilidad ilusoria y fragilidad bajo perturbaciones. El punto de referencia demuestra que los métodos de dirección existentes carecen de confiabilidad para la implementación práctica y propone criterios más claros para el diseño futuro de métodos.

title: MemArchitect: A Policy Driven Memory Governance Layer
summary: MemArchitect introduces a governance layer that addresses gaps in how large language model agents manage memory by enforcing policies for decay, conflict resolution, and privacy. The framework demonstrates that governed memory consistently outperforms unmanaged approaches in autonomous systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.18330v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Persistent Large Language Model (LLM) agents expose a critical governance gap in memory management. Standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks treat memory as passive storage, lacking mechanisms to resolve contradictions, enforce privacy, or prevent outdated information ("zombie memories") from contaminating the context window.
  We introduce MemArchitect, a governance layer that decouples memory lifecycle management from model weights. MemArchitect enforces explicit, rule-based policies, including memory decay, conflict resolution, and privacy controls.
  We demonstrate that governed memory consistently outperforms unmanaged memory in agentic settings, highlighting the necessity of structured memory governance for reliable and safe autonomous systems.

zh.title: MemArchitect：策略驱动的记忆治理层
zh.summary: MemArchitect引入了一个治理层，通过执行衰减、冲突解决和隐私的政策，解决了大语言模型agent在内存管理中的空白。该框架证明了治理内存在自主系统中的性能一致优于无管理的方法。

fr.title: MemArchitect : Une couche de gouvernance de la mémoire pilotée par les politiques
fr.summary: MemArchitect introduit une couche de gouvernance qui comble les lacunes dans la gestion de la mémoire des agents de grands modèles de langage en appliquant des politiques de décroissance, de résolution des conflits et de confidentialité. Le cadre démontre que la mémoire gouvernée surpasse systématiquement les approches non gérées dans les systèmes autonomes.

de.title: MemArchitect: Eine durch Richtlinien gesteuerte Speichergovernance-Schicht
de.summary: MemArchitect führt eine Governance-Schicht ein, die Lücken in der Speicherverwaltung von Large Language Model-Agenten durch Durchsetzung von Richtlinien für Verfall, Konfliktlösung und Datenschutz schließt. Das Framework zeigt, dass verwalteter Speicher in autonomen Systemen konsistent bessere Leistungen erbringt als nicht verwaltete Ansätze.

es.title: MemArchitect: Una capa de gobernanza de memoria impulsada por políticas
es.summary: MemArchitect introduce una capa de gobernanza que aborda las brechas en cómo los agentes de grandes modelos de lenguaje gestionan la memoria al hacer cumplir políticas de decaimiento, resolución de conflictos y privacidad. El marco demuestra que la memoria gobernada supera consistentemente los enfoques no gestionados en sistemas autónomos.

title: Understanding the Theoretical Foundations of Deep Neural Networks through Differential Equations
summary: A survey examines how differential equations provide a theoretical foundation for understanding and improving deep neural networks across model and layer-level perspectives. The work connects architecture design, theoretical analysis, and performance improvements with practical applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.18331v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have achieved remarkable empirical success, yet the absence of a principled theoretical foundation continues to hinder their systematic development. In this survey, we present differential equations as a theoretical foundation for understanding, analyzing, and improving DNNs. We organize the discussion around three guiding questions: i) how differential equations offer a principled understanding of DNN architectures, ii) how tools from differential equations can be used to improve DNN performance in a principled way, and iii) what real-world applications benefit from grounding DNNs in differential equations. We adopt a two-fold perspective spanning the model level, which interprets the whole DNN as a differential equation, and the layer level, which models individual DNN components as differential equations. From these two perspectives, we review how this framework connects model design, theoretical analysis, and performance improvement. We further discuss real-world applications, as well as key challenges and opportunities for future research.

zh.title: 通过微分方程理解深度神经网络的理论基础
zh.summary: 一项调查研究了微分方程如何在模型和层级视角上为理解和改进深度神经网络提供理论基础。该工作将架构设计、理论分析和性能改进与实际应用联系起来。

fr.title: Comprendre les fondements théoriques des réseaux de neurones profonds par les équations différentielles
fr.summary: Une enquête examine comment les équations différentielles fournissent une base théorique pour comprendre et améliorer les réseaux de neurones profonds selon des perspectives au niveau du modèle et de la couche. Le travail relie la conception de l'architecture, l'analyse théorique et les améliorations de performance aux applications pratiques.

de.title: Verständnis der theoretischen Grundlagen tiefer neuronaler Netze durch Differentialgleichungen
de.summary: Eine Umfrage untersucht, wie Differentialgleichungen eine theoretische Grundlage für das Verständnis und die Verbesserung tiefer neuronaler Netze aus Modell- und Schichtperspektiven bieten. Die Arbeit verbindet Architekturdesign, theoretische Analyse und Leistungsverbesserungen mit praktischen Anwendungen.

es.title: Comprender los fundamentos teóricos de las redes neuronales profundas a través de ecuaciones diferenciales
es.summary: Un estudio examina cómo las ecuaciones diferenciales proporcionan una base teórica para entender y mejorar las redes neuronales profundas desde perspectivas de modelo y capa. El trabajo conecta el diseño de arquitectura, el análisis teórico y las mejoras de rendimiento con aplicaciones prácticas.

title: Large-Scale Analysis of Political Propaganda on Moltbook
summary: An NLP study of 673,127 posts on Moltbook, a Reddit-style platform for AI agents, finds political propaganda accounts for 1% of posts but 42% of political content, concentrated in a small number of communities. A minority of agents produce the majority of this propaganda despite limited evidence of comment amplification.
content:
arXiv:2603.18349v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present an NLP-based study of political propaganda on Moltbook, a Reddit-style platform for AI agents. To enable large-scale analysis, we develop LLM-based classifiers to detect political propaganda, validated against expert annotation (Cohen's $\kappa$= 0.64-0.74). Using a dataset of 673,127 posts and 879,606 comments, we find that political propaganda accounts for 1% of all posts and 42% of all political content. These posts are concentrated in a small set of communities, with 70% of such posts falling into five of them. 4% of agents produced 51% of these posts. We further find that a minority of these agents repeatedly post highly similar content within and across communities. Despite this, we find limited evidence that comments amplify political propaganda.

zh.title: Moltbook平台上政治宣传的大规模分析
zh.summary: 一项针对Moltbook（一个类Reddit的AI代理平台）上673,127条帖子的自然语言处理研究发现，政治宣传占帖子总数的1%，但占政治内容的42%，集中在少数几个社区。尽管缺乏评论放大的证据，少数代理产生了大多数这类宣传。

fr.title: Analyse à grande échelle de la propagande politique sur Moltbook
fr.summary: Une étude en traitement du langage naturel portant sur 673 127 messages sur Moltbook, une plateforme de style Reddit pour les agents IA, révèle que la propagande politique représente 1 % des messages mais 42 % du contenu politique, concentrée dans un petit nombre de communautés. Une minorité d'agents produit la majorité de cette propagande malgré des preuves limitées d'amplification des commentaires.

de.title: Großangelegte Analyse von politischer Propaganda auf Moltbook
de.summary: Eine NLP-Studie von 673.127 Beiträgen auf Moltbook, einer Reddit-ähnlichen Plattform für KI-Agenten, zeigt, dass politische Propaganda 1 % der Beiträge ausmacht, aber 42 % des politischen Inhalts ausmacht und in einer kleinen Anzahl von Gemeinschaften konzentriert ist. Eine Minderheit von Agenten produziert die Mehrheit dieser Propaganda trotz begrenzter Beweise für Kommentarverstärkung.

es.title: Análisis a gran escala de propaganda política en Moltbook
es.summary: Un estudio de PNL de 673.127 publicaciones en Moltbook, una plataforma de estilo Reddit para agentes de IA, encuentra que la propaganda política representa el 1% de las publicaciones pero el 42% del contenido político, concentrada en un pequeño número de comunidades. Una minoría de agentes produce la mayoría de esta propaganda a pesar de la limitada evidencia de amplificación de comentarios.

title: Interpretability without actionability: mechanistic methods cannot correct language model errors despite near-perfect internal representations
summary: Research comparing four mechanistic interpretability methods finds that while language models contain internal knowledge to correct errors, current methods cannot reliably translate this knowledge into corrected outputs. The 53-percentage-point gap between internal knowledge and performance raises concerns for AI safety approaches assuming interpretability enables error correction.
content:
arXiv:2603.18353v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Language models encode task-relevant knowledge in internal representations that far exceeds their output performance, but whether mechanistic interpretability methods can bridge this knowledge-action gap has not been systematically tested. We compared four mechanistic interpretability methods -- concept bottleneck steering (Steerling-8B), sparse autoencoder feature steering, logit lens with activation patching, and linear probing with truthfulness separator vector steering (Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct) -- for correcting false-negative triage errors using 400 physician-adjudicated clinical vignettes (144 hazards, 256 benign). Linear probes discriminated hazardous from benign cases with 98.2% AUROC, yet the model's output sensitivity was only 45.1%, a 53-percentage-point knowledge-action gap. Concept bottleneck steering corrected 20% of missed hazards but disrupted 53% of correct detections, indistinguishable from random perturbation (p=0.84). SAE feature steering produced zero effect despite 3,695 significant features. TSV steering at high strength corrected 24% of missed hazards while disrupting 6% of correct detections, but left 76% of errors uncorrected. Current mechanistic interpretability methods cannot reliably translate internal knowledge into corrected outputs, with implications for AI safety frameworks that assume interpretability enables effective error correction.

zh.title: 可解释性而非可行性：机械解释方法尽管具有接近完美的内部表示，但无法纠正语言模型的错误
zh.summary: 比较四种机械解释方法的研究发现，虽然语言模型包含纠正错误的内部知识，但当前方法无法可靠地将这些知识转化为纠正后的输出。内部知识与性能之间的53个百分点差距引发了对假设可解释性能够实现错误纠正的人工智能安全方法的担忧。

fr.title: Interprétabilité sans caractère actionnable : les méthodes mécanistiques ne peuvent pas corriger les erreurs du modèle de langage malgré des représentations internes quasi-parfaites
fr.summary: Une recherche comparant quatre méthodes d'interprétabilité mécanistique constate que bien que les modèles de langage contiennent des connaissances internes pour corriger les erreurs, les méthodes actuelles ne peuvent pas traduire de manière fiable ces connaissances en résultats corrigés. L'écart de 53 points de pourcentage entre les connaissances internes et les performances soulève des préoccupations concernant les approches de sécurité de l'IA supposant que l'interprétabilité permet la correction des erreurs.

de.title: Interpretierbarkeit ohne Handlungsfähigkeit: Mechanistische Methoden können Sprachmodellfehler trotz nahezu perfekter interner Darstellungen nicht korrigieren
de.summary: Eine Forschung, die vier mechanistische Interpretierungsmethoden vergleicht, stellt fest, dass zwar Sprachmodelle internes Wissen zur Fehlerkorrektur enthalten, aktuelle Methoden dieses Wissen jedoch nicht zuverlässig in korrigierte Ausgaben umsetzen können. Die 53-Prozentpunkt-Lücke zwischen internem Wissen und Leistung wirft Bedenken bezüglich KI-Sicherheitsansätze auf, die davon ausgehen, dass Interpretierbarkeit Fehlerkorrektur ermöglicht.

es.title: Interpretabilidad sin capacidad de acción: los métodos mecanicistas no pueden corregir errores del modelo de lenguaje a pesar de representaciones internas casi perfectas
es.summary: La investigación que compara cuatro métodos de interpretabilidad mecanicista encuentra que aunque los modelos de lenguaje contienen conocimiento interno para corregir errores, los métodos actuales no pueden traducir de manera confiable este conocimiento en salidas corregidas. La brecha de 53 puntos porcentuales entre el conocimiento interno y el rendimiento genera preocupaciones sobre los enfoques de seguridad de la IA que asumen que la interpretabilidad permite la corrección de errores.

title: LGESynthNet: Controlled Scar Synthesis for Improved Scar Segmentation in Cardiac LGE-MRI Imaging
summary: LGESynthNet uses latent diffusion models to synthetically generate cardiac MRI enhancement images with precise control over size, location, and extent, trained on just 429 images. When used for data augmentation, the synthetic samples improve downstream segmentation by up to 6 points and detection by 20 points.
content:
arXiv:2603.18356v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Segmentation of enhancement in LGE cardiac MRI is critical for diagnosing various ischemic and non-ischemic cardiomyopathies. However, creating pixel-level annotations for these images is challenging and labor-intensive, leading to limited availability of annotated data. Generative models, particularly diffusion models, offer promise for synthetic data generation, yet many rely on large training datasets and often struggle with fine-grained conditioning control, especially for small or localized features. We introduce LGESynthNet, a latent diffusion-based framework for controllable enhancement synthesis, enabling explicit control over size, location, and transmural extent. Formulated as inpainting using a ControlNet-based architecture, the model integrates: (a) a reward model for conditioning-specific supervision, (b) a captioning module for anatomically descriptive text prompts, and (c) a biomedical text encoder. Trained on just 429 images (79 patients), it produces realistic, anatomically coherent samples. A quality control filter selects outputs with high conditioning-fidelity, which when used for training augmentation, improve downstream segmentation and detection performance, by up-to 6 and 20 points respectively.

zh.title: LGESynthNet：用于心脏LGE-MRI成像中疤痕分割改进的受控疤痕合成
zh.summary: LGESynthNet使用潜在扩散模型合成生成心脏MRI增强图像，精确控制大小、位置和范围，仅用429张图像训练。当用于数据增强时，合成样本可将下游分割改进高达6个百分点，检测改进20个百分点。

fr.title: LGESynthNet : Synthèse contrôlée de cicatrices pour l'amélioration de la segmentation des cicatrices en imagerie cardiaque LGE-IRM
fr.summary: LGESynthNet utilise des modèles de diffusion latente pour générer synthétiquement des images d'amélioration IRM cardiaque avec un contrôle précis de la taille, de la localisation et de l'étendue, entraîné sur seulement 429 images. Lorsqu'il est utilisé pour l'augmentation de données, les échantillons synthétiques améliorent la segmentation en aval jusqu'à 6 points et la détection de 20 points.

de.title: LGESynthNet: Kontrollierte Narbensynthese für verbesserte Narbensegmentierung in der Herz-LGE-MRT-Bildgebung
de.summary: LGESynthNet verwendet latente Diffusionsmodelle zur synthetischen Erzeugung von Kardio-MRT-Verbesserungsbildern mit präziser Kontrolle über Größe, Position und Umfang, trainiert an nur 429 Bildern. Bei Verwendung zur Datenvermehrung verbessern die synthetischen Proben die nachgelagerte Segmentierung um bis zu 6 Punkte und die Erkennung um 20 Punkte.

es.title: LGESynthNet: Síntesis controlada de cicatrices para mejorar la segmentación de cicatrices en imagen cardíaca LGE-RMN
es.summary: LGESynthNet utiliza modelos de difusión latente para generar sintéticamente imágenes de realce de RMN cardíaca con control preciso sobre tamaño, ubicación y extensión, entrenado con solo 429 imágenes. Cuando se utiliza para aumento de datos, las muestras sintéticas mejoran la segmentación descendente hasta 6 puntos y la detección en 20 puntos.

title: From Weak Cues to Real Identities: Evaluating Inference-Driven De-Anonymization in LLM Agents
summary: A study demonstrates that LLM-based agents can autonomously reconstruct real-world identities from scattered, non-identifying cues combined with public information, achieving 79.2% reconstruction accuracy in the Netflix Prize scenario. The findings establish identity inference as a first-class privacy risk extending beyond traditional anonymization safeguards.
content:
arXiv:2603.18382v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Anonymization is widely treated as a practical safeguard because re-identifying anonymous records was historically costly, requiring domain expertise, tailored algorithms, and manual corroboration. We study a growing privacy risk that may weaken this barrier: LLM-based agents can autonomously reconstruct real-world identities from scattered, individually non-identifying cues. By combining these sparse cues with public information, agents resolve identities without bespoke engineering. We formalize this threat as \emph{inference-driven linkage} and systematically evaluate it across three settings: classical linkage scenarios (Netflix and AOL), \emph{InferLink} (a controlled benchmark varying task intent, shared cues, and attacker knowledge), and modern text-rich artifacts. Without task-specific heuristics, agents successfully execute both fixed-pool matching and open-ended identity resolution. In the Netflix Prize setting, an agent reconstructs 79.2\% of identities, significantly outperforming a 56.0\% classical baseline. Furthermore, linkage emerges not only under explicit adversarial prompts but also as a byproduct of benign cross-source analysis in \emph{InferLink} and unstructured research narratives. These findings establish that identity inference -- not merely explicit information disclosure -- must be treated as a first-class privacy risk; evaluations must measure what identities an agent can infer.

zh.title: 从弱线索到真实身份：评估大语言模型代理中推理驱动的去匿名化
zh.summary: 一项研究表明，基于大语言模型的代理可以自主地从分散的、非识别性线索结合公开信息中重构真实身份，在Netflix Prize场景中达到79.2%的重构准确率。这些发现将身份推理确立为超越传统匿名化保障的一流隐私风险。

fr.title: Des indices faibles aux identités réelles : Évaluation de la dé-anonymisation pilotée par l'inférence dans les agents LLM
fr.summary: Une étude démontre que les agents basés sur LLM peuvent autonomement reconstruire des identités du monde réel à partir d'indices épars et non-identifiants combinés avec des informations publiques, atteignant une précision de reconstruction de 79,2 % dans le scénario Netflix Prize. Les résultats établissent l'inférence d'identité comme un risque de confidentialité de première classe s'étendant au-delà des garanties d'anonymisation traditionnelles.

de.title: Von schwachen Hinweisen zu echten Identitäten: Bewertung der Inferenz-gesteuerten De-Anonymisierung bei LLM-Agenten
de.summary: Eine Studie zeigt, dass auf LLM basierende Agenten eigenständig reale Identitäten aus verstreuten, nicht identifizierenden Hinweisen in Kombination mit öffentlichen Informationen rekonstruieren können und eine Rekonstruktionsgenauigkeit von 79,2 % im Netflix-Prize-Szenario erreichen. Die Ergebnisse etablieren Identitätsinferenz als erstklassiges Datenschutzrisiko, das über traditionelle Anonymisierungsschutzmaßnahmen hinausgeht.

es.title: De pistas débiles a identidades reales: Evaluación de la des-anonimización impulsada por inferencia en agentes LLM
es.summary: Un estudio demuestra que los agentes basados en LLM pueden reconstruir autónomamente identidades del mundo real a partir de pistas dispersas y no identificativas combinadas con información pública, logrando una precisión de reconstrucción del 79,2 % en el escenario Netflix Prize. Los hallazgos establecen la inferencia de identidad como un riesgo de privacidad de primera categoría que se extiende más allá de las garantías tradicionales de anonimización.

title: Reflection in the Dark: Exposing and Escaping the Black Box in Reflective Prompt Optimization
summary: VISTA, a new multi-agent automatic prompt optimization framework, addresses limitations in black-box reflective methods by enabling interpretable optimization trajectories and escaping local optima. The method recovers from severe performance degradation and consistently outperforms baselines across multiple benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.18388v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Automatic prompt optimization (APO) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for improving LLM performance without manual prompt engineering. Reflective APO methods such as GEPA iteratively refine prompts by diagnosing failure cases, but the optimization process remains black-box and label-free, leading to uninterpretable trajectories and systematic failure. We identify and empirically demonstrate four limitations: on GSM8K with a defective seed, GEPA degrades accuracy from 23.81% to 13.50%. We propose VISTA, a multi-agent APO framework that decouples hypothesis generation from prompt rewriting, enabling semantically labeled hypotheses, parallel minibatch verification, and interpretable optimization trace. A two-layer explore-exploit mechanism combining random restart and epsilon-greedy sampling further escapes local optima. VISTA recovers accuracy to 87.57% on the same defective seed and consistently outperforms baselines across all conditions on GSM8K and AIME2025.

zh.title: 黑暗中的反思：在反射性提示词优化中暴露和逃脱黑箱
zh.summary: VISTA是一个新的多代理自动提示词优化框架，通过启用可解释的优化轨迹和逃脱局部最优来解决黑箱反射性方法的局限性。该方法可以从严重的性能降级中恢复，并在多个基准测试中始终优于基准方法。

fr.title: Réflexion dans l'obscurité : Exposer et s'échapper de la boîte noire dans l'optimisation des invites réfléchies
fr.summary: VISTA, un nouveau cadre d'optimisation automatique des invites multi-agents, résout les limitations des méthodes réfléchies de boîte noire en permettant des trajectoires d'optimisation interprétables et en échappant aux optima locaux. La méthode se rétablit d'une dégradation sévère des performances et surpasse systématiquement les références sur plusieurs benchmarks.

de.title: Reflexion in der Dunkelheit: Aufdecken und Flucht aus der Black Box bei reflexiver Prompt-Optimierung
de.summary: VISTA ist ein neues Multi-Agent-Framework zur automatischen Prompt-Optimierung, das die Einschränkungen schwarzer Box-Reflexionsmethoden durch interpretierbare Optimierungstrajektorien und Flucht vor lokalen Optima behebt. Die Methode erholt sich von schwerer Leistungsbeeinträchtigung und übertrifft konsequent Baselines über mehrere Benchmarks hinweg.
