es.title: Reflexión en la oscuridad: Exponer y escapar de la caja negra en la optimización de indicaciones reflexivas
es.summary: VISTA, un nuevo marco de optimización automática de indicaciones multi-agente, aborda las limitaciones de los métodos reflexivos de caja negra al permitir trayectorias de optimización interpretables y escapar de óptimos locales. El método se recupera de la degradación severa del rendimiento y supera consistentemente las líneas base en múltiples puntos de referencia.

title: From Topic to Transition Structure: Unsupervised Concept Discovery at Corpus Scale via Predictive Associative Memory
summary: Researchers train a 29.4M-parameter model on 373 million co-occurrence pairs from classic literature to discover transition-structure concepts—patterns in what text does rather than its content. The multi-resolution concept map reveals corpus-wide patterns from broad literary modes to precise scene templates that transfer to unseen novels.
content:
arXiv:2603.18420v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Embedding models group text by semantic content, what text is about. We show that temporal co-occurrence within texts discovers a different kind of structure: recurrent transition-structure concepts or what text does. We train a 29.4M-parameter contrastive model on 373 million co-occurrence pairs from 9,766 Project Gutenberg texts (24.96 million passages), mapping pre-trained embeddings into an association space where passages with similar transition structure cluster together. Under capacity constraint (42.75% accuracy), the model must compress across recurring patterns rather than memorise individual co-occurrences. Clustering at six granularities (k=50 to k=2,000) produces a multi-resolution concept map; from broad modes like "direct confrontation" and "lyrical meditation" to precise registers and scene templates like "sailor dialect" and "courtroom cross-examination." At k=100, clusters average 4,508 books each (of 9,766), confirming corpus-wide patterns. Direct comparison with embedding-similarity clustering shows that raw embeddings group by topic while association-space clusters group by function, register, and literary tradition. Unseen novels are assigned to existing clusters without retraining; the association model concentrates each novel into a selective subset of coherent clusters, while raw embedding assignment saturates nearly all clusters. Validation controls address positional, length, and book-concentration confounds. The method extends Predictive Associative Memory (PAM, arXiv:2602.11322) from episodic recall to concept formation: where PAM recalls specific associations, multi-epoch contrastive training under compression extracts structural patterns that transfer to unseen texts, the same framework producing qualitatively different behaviour in a different regime.

zh.title: 从话题到转变结构：通过预测关联记忆在语料库规模上进行无监督概念发现
zh.summary: 研究人员在包含3.73亿个共现对的经典文学语料库上训练一个2940万参数的模型，以发现转变结构概念——即文本表现方式而非内容中的模式。多分辨率概念图揭示了从宽泛的文学模式到精确场景模板的语料库范围模式，这些模式能够转移到未见过的小说中。

fr.title: Du sujet à la structure de transition : découverte de concepts non supervisés à l'échelle du corpus via mémoire associative prédictive
fr.summary: Les chercheurs entraînent un modèle de 29,4 millions de paramètres sur 373 millions de paires de co-occurrence provenant de littérature classique pour découvrir des concepts de structure de transition—des modèles dans ce que le texte fait plutôt que son contenu. La carte conceptuelle multi-résolution révèle des modèles à l'échelle du corpus, des modes littéraires généraux aux modèles de scènes précis qui se transfèrent à des romans non observés.

de.title: Vom Thema zur Übergangsstruktur: Unbeaufsichtigte Konzeptentdeckung im Corpus-Maßstab durch prädiktives assoziatives Gedächtnis
de.summary: Forscher trainieren ein Modell mit 29,4 Millionen Parametern auf 373 Millionen Co-Occurrence-Paaren aus klassischer Literatur, um Übergangsstrukturkonzepte zu entdecken—Muster in dem, was Text tut, statt seinen Inhalt. Die Multi-Resolutions-Konzeptkarte offenbart korpusweite Muster von breiten literarischen Modi bis zu präzisen Szenenschablonen, die auf ungesehene Romane übertragen werden.

es.title: Del tema a la estructura de transición: descubrimiento de conceptos no supervisados a escala de corpus mediante memoria asociativa predictiva
es.summary: Los investigadores entrenan un modelo de 29,4 millones de parámetros en 373 millones de pares de co-ocurrencia de literatura clásica para descubrir conceptos de estructura de transición—patrones en lo que el texto hace en lugar de su contenido. El mapa conceptual multiresolución revela patrones a escala de corpus, desde modos literarios amplios hasta plantillas de escenas precisas que se transfieren a novelas no vistas.

title: Prune-then-Quantize or Quantize-then-Prune? Understanding the Impact of Compression Order in Joint Model Compression
summary: A study investigates whether the order of applying compression methods like pruning and quantization affects model performance, introducing the Progressive Intensity Hypothesis that weaker perturbations should precede stronger ones. Experiments on language and vision models validate this principle across multiple compression strategies and setups.
content:
arXiv:2603.18426v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: What happens when multiple compression methods are combined-does the order in which they are applied matter? Joint model compression has emerged as a powerful strategy to achieve higher efficiency by combining multiple methods such as pruning and quantization. A central but underexplored factor in joint model compression is the compression order, or the sequence of different methods within the compression pipeline. Most prior studies have either sidestepped the issue by assuming orthogonality between techniques, while a few have examined them only in highly constrained cases. Consequently, the broader role of compression order in shaping model performance remains poorly understood. In this paper, we address the overlooked problem of compression order and provide both theoretical and empirical analysis. We formulate the problem of optimizing the compression order and introduce the Progressive Intensity Hypothesis, which states that weaker perturbations should precede stronger ones. We provide theoretical guarantees showing that the relative benefit of one order increases with the underlying performance gap. Extensive experiments on both language and vision models validate the hypothesis, and further show its generality to broader setups such as multi-stage compression and mixed-precision quantization.

zh.title: 剪枝-然后-量化还是量化-然后-剪枝？理解联合模型压缩中压缩顺序的影响
zh.summary: 一项研究调查了应用剪枝和量化等压缩方法的顺序是否会影响模型性能，引入了渐进强度假说，即较弱的扰动应该先于较强的扰动。在语言和视觉模型上的实验验证了这一原则在多种压缩策略和设置中的有效性。

fr.title: Élagage puis quantification ou quantification puis élagage ? Comprendre l'impact de l'ordre de compression dans la compression conjointe de modèles
fr.summary: Une étude enquête sur la question de savoir si l'ordre d'application de méthodes de compression comme l'élagage et la quantification affecte les performances du modèle, introduisant l'hypothèse d'intensité progressive selon laquelle les perturbations plus faibles doivent précéder les plus fortes. Les expériences sur les modèles de langage et de vision valident ce principe dans plusieurs stratégies de compression et configurations.

de.title: Pruning-dann-Quantisierung oder Quantisierung-dann-Pruning? Verständnis der Auswirkungen der Kompressionsreihenfolge bei der gemeinsamen Modellkompression
de.summary: Eine Studie untersucht, ob die Reihenfolge der Anwendung von Kompressionsmethoden wie Pruning und Quantisierung die Modellleistung beeinflusst, und führt die Progressive-Intensitäts-Hypothese ein, wonach schwächere Störungen stärkere vorangehen sollten. Experimente an Sprach- und Sichtmodellen validieren dieses Prinzip über mehrere Kompressionsstrategien und Setups.

es.title: ¿Poda y luego cuantización o cuantización y luego poda? Comprender el impacto del orden de compresión en la compresión conjunta de modelos
es.summary: Un estudio investiga si el orden de aplicación de métodos de compresión como poda y cuantización afecta el rendimiento del modelo, introduciendo la Hipótesis de Intensidad Progresiva que establece que las perturbaciones más débiles deben preceder a las más fuertes. Los experimentos en modelos de lenguaje y visión validan este principio en múltiples estrategias de compresión y configuraciones.

title: AS2 -- Attention-Based Soft Answer Sets: An End-to-End Differentiable Neuro-Soft-Symbolic Reasoning Architecture
summary: AS2 introduces a fully differentiable neuro-symbolic architecture that replaces discrete solvers with soft approximations of Answer Set Programming, enabling end-to-end training through constraint checks. The system achieves 99.89% accuracy on Visual Sudoku and above 99.7% digit accuracy on multi-addend MNIST addition.
content:
arXiv:2603.18436v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Neuro-symbolic artificial intelligence (AI) systems typically couple a neural perception module to a discrete symbolic solver through a non-differentiable boundary, preventing constraint-satisfaction feedback from reaching the perception encoder during training. We introduce AS2 (Attention-Based Soft Answer Sets), a fully differentiable neuro-symbolic architecture that replaces the discrete solver with a soft, continuous approximation of the Answer Set Programming (ASP) immediate consequence operator $T_P$. AS2 maintains per-position probability distributions over a finite symbol domain throughout the forward pass and trains end-to-end by minimizing the fixed-point residual of a probabilistic lift of $T_P$, thereby differentiating through the constraint check without invoking an external solver at either training or inference time. The architecture is entirely free of conventional positional embeddings. Instead, it encodes problem structure through constraint-group membership embeddings that directly reflect the declarative ASP specification, making the model agnostic to arbitrary position indexing. On Visual Sudoku, AS2 achieves 99.89% cell accuracy and 100% constraint satisfaction (verified by Clingo) across 1,000 test boards, using a greedy constrained decoding procedure that requires no external solver. On MNIST Addition with $N \in \{2, 4, 8\}$ addends, AS2 achieves digit accuracy above 99.7% across all scales. These results demonstrate that a soft differentiable fixpoint operator, combined with constraint-aware attention and declarative constraint specification, can match or exceed pipeline and solver-based neuro-symbolic systems while maintaining full end-to-end differentiability.

zh.title: AS2——基于注意力的软答案集：端到端可微神经符号推理架构
zh.summary: AS2引入了一个完全可微的神经符号架构，用软答案集编程的近似值替换离散求解器，实现通过约束检查的端到端训练。该系统在视觉数独上实现了99.89%的准确率，在多加数MNIST加法上实现了99.7%以上的数字准确率。

fr.title: AS2 — Ensembles de réponses douces basés sur l'attention : une architecture de raisonnement neuro-doux-symbolique différentiable de bout en bout
fr.summary: AS2 introduit une architecture neuro-symbolique entièrement différentiable qui remplace les solveurs discrets par des approximations douces de la programmation d'ensembles de réponses, permettant un entraînement de bout en bout grâce aux vérifications de contraintes. Le système atteint une précision de 99,89% sur le Sudoku visuel et plus de 99,7% de précision des chiffres sur l'addition multi-addends MNIST.

de.title: AS2 — Aufmerksamkeitsbasierte weiche Antwortmengen: Eine End-to-End-differenzierbare neuro-soft-symbolische Reasoning-Architektur
de.summary: AS2 führt eine vollständig differenzierbare neuro-symbolische Architektur ein, die diskrete Löser durch weiche Annäherungen an Answer Set Programming ersetzt und End-to-End-Training durch Constraint-Checks ermöglicht. Das System erreicht eine Genauigkeit von 99,89% auf Visual Sudoku und eine Zifferngenauigkeit von über 99,7% bei der Multi-Addend-MNIST-Addition.

es.title: AS2 — Conjuntos de respuestas suaves basados en atención: una arquitectura de razonamiento neuro-suave-simbólica diferenciable de extremo a extremo
es.summary: AS2 introduce una arquitectura neuro-simbólica completamente diferenciable que reemplaza los solucionadores discretos con aproximaciones suaves de la Programación de Conjuntos de Respuestas, permitiendo el entrenamiento de extremo a extremo a través de verificaciones de restricciones. El sistema logra una precisión del 99,89% en Visual Sudoku y una precisión de dígitos superior al 99,7% en la suma MNIST de múltiples sumandos.

title: AlignMamba-2: Enhancing Multimodal Fusion and Sentiment Analysis with Modality-Aware Mamba
summary: AlignMamba-2 proposes a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with modality-aware design to efficiently handle heterogeneous multimodal data for sentiment analysis and fusion. The framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on four benchmarks while maintaining computational efficiency compared to Transformer-based approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.18462v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In the era of large-scale pre-trained models, effectively adapting general knowledge to specific affective computing tasks remains a challenge, particularly regarding computational efficiency and multimodal heterogeneity. While Transformer-based methods have excelled at modeling inter-modal dependencies, their quadratic computational complexity limits their use with long-sequence data. Mamba-based models have emerged as a computationally efficient alternative; however, their inherent sequential scanning mechanism struggles to capture the global, non-sequential relationships that are crucial for effective cross-modal alignment. To address these limitations, we propose \textbf{AlignMamba-2}, an effective and efficient framework for multimodal fusion and sentiment analysis. Our approach introduces a dual alignment strategy that regularizes the model using both Optimal Transport distance and Maximum Mean Discrepancy, promoting geometric and statistical consistency between modalities without incurring any inference-time overhead. More importantly, we design a Modality-Aware Mamba layer, which employs a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with modality-specific and modality-shared experts to explicitly handle data heterogeneity during the fusion process. Extensive experiments on four challenging benchmarks, including dynamic time-series (on the CMU-MOSI and CMU-MOSEI datasets) and static image-related tasks (on the NYU-Depth V2 and MVSA-Single datasets), demonstrate that AlignMamba-2 establishes a new state-of-the-art in both effectiveness and efficiency across diverse pattern recognition tasks, ranging from dynamic time-series analysis to static image-text classification.

zh.title: AlignMamba-2：通过模态感知Mamba增强多模态融合和情感分析
zh.summary: AlignMamba-2提出了一种混合专家架构，具有模态感知设计，可高效处理异构多模态数据用于情感分析和融合。该框架在四个基准上达到最先进的性能，同时与基于Transformer的方法相比保持计算效率。

fr.title: AlignMamba-2 : Améliorer la fusion multimodale et l'analyse des sentiments avec Mamba conscient de la modalité
fr.summary: AlignMamba-2 propose une architecture Mixture-of-Experts avec une conception consciente de la modalité pour traiter efficacement les données multimodales hétérogènes pour l'analyse et la fusion des sentiments. Le cadre atteint les performances de pointe sur quatre repères tout en maintenant l'efficacité computationnelle par rapport aux approches basées sur Transformer.

de.title: AlignMamba-2: Verbesserung der multimodalen Fusion und Sentiment-Analyse mit modalitätsgerechtem Mamba
de.summary: AlignMamba-2 schlägt eine Mixture-of-Experts-Architektur mit modalitätsbewusstem Design vor, um heterogene multimodale Daten für Sentiment-Analyse und Fusion effizient zu verarbeiten. Das Framework erreicht State-of-the-Art-Leistung auf vier Benchmarks und behält gleichzeitig Rechnereffizienz gegenüber Transformer-basierten Ansätzen bei.

es.title: AlignMamba-2: Mejorando la fusión multimodal y análisis de sentimientos con Mamba consciente de modalidad
es.summary: AlignMamba-2 propone una arquitectura Mixture-of-Experts con diseño consciente de modalidad para manejar eficientemente datos multimodales heterogéneos para análisis y fusión de sentimientos. El marco logra un rendimiento de última generación en cuatro puntos de referencia mientras mantiene la eficiencia computacional en comparación con enfoques basados en Transformers.

title: Cognitive Mismatch in Multimodal Large Language Models for Discrete Symbol Understanding
summary: A benchmark study reveals that top-tier multimodal language models struggle with discrete symbol understanding—mathematical formulas, chemical structures, linguistic characters—despite succeeding at complex reasoning tasks. The findings suggest models rely on linguistic probability rather than true visual perception, exposing a significant capability gap.
content:
arXiv:2603.18472v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable success in interpreting natural scenes, their ability to process discrete symbols -- the fundamental building blocks of human cognition -- remains a critical open question. Unlike continuous visual data, symbols such as mathematical formulas, chemical structures, and linguistic characters require precise, deeper interpretation. This paper introduces a comprehensive benchmark to evaluate how top-tier MLLMs navigate these "discrete semantic spaces" across five domains: language, culture, mathematics, physics, and chemistry. Our investigation uncovers a counterintuitive phenomenon: models often fail at basic symbol recognition yet succeed in complex reasoning tasks, suggesting they rely on linguistic probability rather than true visual perception. By exposing this "cognitive mismatch", we highlight a significant gap in current AI capabilities: the struggle to truly perceive and understand the symbolic languages that underpin scientific discovery and abstract thought. This work offers a roadmap for developing more rigorous, human-aligned intelligent systems.

zh.title: 多模态大语言模型中离散符号理解的认知失配
zh.summary: 一项基准研究显示，顶级多模态语言模型在离散符号理解（数学公式、化学结构、语言字符）方面存在困难，尽管在复杂推理任务中表现出色。研究结果表明模型依赖语言概率而非真实视觉感知，暴露了一个显著的能力差距。

fr.title: Décalage cognitif dans les grands modèles de langage multimodaux pour la compréhension des symboles discrets
fr.summary: Une étude de référence révèle que les modèles de langage multimodaux de premier ordre ont du mal à comprendre les symboles discrets—formules mathématiques, structures chimiques, caractères linguistiques—malgré leur succès dans les tâches de raisonnement complexe. Les résultats suggèrent que les modèles s'appuient sur la probabilité linguistique plutôt que sur une véritable perception visuelle, exposant un écart de capacité important.

de.title: Kognitiver Mismatch in multimodalen großen Sprachmodellen für das Verständnis diskreter Symbole
de.summary: Eine Benchmark-Studie zeigt, dass hochwertige multimodale Sprachmodelle Schwierigkeiten mit dem Verständnis diskreter Symbole haben—mathematische Formeln, chemische Strukturen, sprachliche Zeichen—trotz ihres Erfolgs bei komplexen Aufgaben zur Argumentation. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass Modelle auf sprachliche Wahrscheinlichkeit anstelle echter visueller Wahrnehmung angewiesen sind und eine erhebliche Fähigkeitslücke aufdecken.

es.title: Desajuste cognitivo en modelos de lenguaje grandes multimodales para la comprensión de símbolos discretos
es.summary: Un estudio de referencia revela que los modelos de lenguaje multimodal de primer nivel tienen dificultades con la comprensión de símbolos discretos—fórmulas matemáticas, estructuras químicas, caracteres lingüísticos—a pesar de tener éxito en tareas de razonamiento complejo. Los hallazgos sugieren que los modelos se basan en la probabilidad lingüística en lugar de la verdadera percepción visual, exponiendo una brecha de capacidad significativa.

title: Cross-Domain Demo-to-Code via Neurosymbolic Counterfactual Reasoning
summary: NeSyCR, a neurosymbolic framework, enables robotic agents to adapt video demonstrations across different domains by recognizing and resolving procedural mismatches through counterfactual reasoning. The approach achieves 31.14% improvement over baseline methods in cross-domain robotic manipulation tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.18495v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent advances in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have enabled video-instructed robotic programming, allowing agents to interpret video demonstrations and generate executable control code. We formulate video-instructed robotic programming as a cross-domain adaptation problem, where perceptual and physical differences between demonstration and deployment induce procedural mismatches. However, current VLMs lack the procedural understanding needed to reformulate causal dependencies and achieve task-compatible behavior under such domain shifts. We introduce NeSyCR, a neurosymbolic counterfactual reasoning framework that enables verifiable adaptation of task procedures, providing a reliable synthesis of code policies. NeSyCR abstracts video demonstrations into symbolic trajectories that capture the underlying task procedure. Given deployment observations, it derives counterfactual states that reveal cross-domain incompatibilities. By exploring the symbolic state space with verifiable checks, NeSyCR proposes procedural revisions that restore compatibility with the demonstrated procedure. NeSyCR achieves a 31.14% improvement in task success over the strongest baseline Statler, showing robust cross-domain adaptation across both simulated and real-world manipulation tasks.

zh.title: 通过神经符号反事实推理的跨域演示转代码
zh.summary: NeSyCR是一个神经符号框架，通过反事实推理识别和解决程序不匹配，使机器人代理能够在不同领域中适应视频演示。该方法在跨域机器人操作任务中相比基准方法实现了31.14%的改进。

fr.title: Conversion de démonstrations en code entre domaines via raisonnement contrefactuel neurosymbolique
fr.summary: NeSyCR, un framework neurosymbolique, permet aux agents robotiques d'adapter les démonstrations vidéo entre différents domaines en reconnaissant et en résolvant les incompatibilités procédurales par le biais du raisonnement contrefactuel. L'approche réalise une amélioration de 31,14% par rapport aux méthodes de référence dans les tâches de manipulation robotique entre domaines.

de.title: Domänenübergreifende Demo-zu-Code-Umwandlung durch neurosymbolische kontrafaktische Argumentation
de.summary: NeSyCR, ein neurosymbolisches Framework, ermöglicht es Robotagenten, Videodemonstrationen domänenübergreifend anzupassen, indem prozedurale Nichtübereinstimmungen durch kontrafaktische Argumentation erkannt und gelöst werden. Der Ansatz erreicht eine Verbesserung von 31,14% gegenüber Baseline-Methoden bei domänenübergreifenden Robotemanipulationsaufgaben.

es.title: Demostración a código entre dominios mediante razonamiento contrafáctico neurosimbólico
es.summary: NeSyCR, un marco neurosimbólico, permite que los agentes robóticos adapten demostraciones de video en diferentes dominios reconociendo y resolviendo desajustes procedimentales mediante razonamiento contrafáctico. El enfoque logra una mejora del 31,14% sobre métodos de línea base en tareas de manipulación robótica entre dominios.

title: Expert Personas Improve LLM Alignment but Damage Accuracy: Bootstrapping Intent-Based Persona Routing with PRISM
summary: Expert personas in LLMs improve alignment and safety but can reduce accuracy on general tasks; the gap depends on model type and prompt design. Researchers developed PRISM, a framework using intent-based persona routing that maintains alignment benefits without sacrificing discriminative task performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.18507v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Persona prompting can steer LLM generation towards a domain-specific tone and pattern. This behavior enables use cases in multi-agent systems where diverse interactions are crucial and human-centered tasks require high-level human alignment. Prior works provide mixed opinions on their utility: some report performance gains when using expert personas for certain domains and their contribution to data diversity in synthetic data creation, while others find near-zero or negative impact on general utility. To fully leverage the benefits of the LLM persona and avoid its harmfulness, a more comprehensive investigation of the mechanism is crucial. In this work, we study how model optimization, task type, prompt length, and placement can impact expert persona effectiveness across instruction-tuned and reasoning LLMs, and provide insight into conditions under which expert personas fail and succeed. Based on our findings, we developed a pipeline to fully leverage the benefits of an expert persona, named PRISM (Persona Routing via Intent-based Self-Modeling), which self-distills an intent-conditioned expert persona into a gated LoRA adapter through a bootstrapping process that requires no external data, models, or knowledge. PRISM enhances human preference and safety alignment on generative tasks while maintaining accuracy on discriminative tasks across all models, with minimal memory and computing overhead.

zh.title: 专家角色改进LLM对齐但损害准确性：使用PRISM进行基于意图的角色路由引导
zh.summary: LLM中的专家角色改进了对齐和安全性，但可能降低一般任务的准确性；差距取决于模型类型和提示设计。研究人员开发了PRISM，这是一个使用基于意图的角色路由的框架，可以保持对齐优势而不牺牲判别性任务性能。

fr.title: Les personas experts améliorent l'alignement des LLM mais endommagent la précision : Amorçage du routage de personas basé sur l'intention avec PRISM
fr.summary: Les personas experts dans les LLM améliorent l'alignement et la sécurité mais peuvent réduire la précision sur les tâches générales ; l'écart dépend du type de modèle et de la conception des invites. Les chercheurs ont développé PRISM, un framework utilisant le routage de personas basé sur l'intention qui maintient les avantages d'alignement sans sacrifier les performances des tâches discriminantes.

de.title: Expert-Personas verbessern die LLM-Ausrichtung, schädigen aber die Genauigkeit: Bootstrapping Intent-Based Persona Routing mit PRISM
de.summary: Expert-Personas in LLMs verbessern die Ausrichtung und Sicherheit, können aber die Genauigkeit bei allgemeinen Aufgaben verringern; die Lücke hängt vom Modelltyp und Prompt-Design ab. Forscher entwickelten PRISM, ein Framework, das Intent-basiertes Persona-Routing verwendet und die Vorteile der Ausrichtung beibehält, ohne die Leistung diskriminativer Aufgaben zu opfern.

es.title: Personas de expertos mejoran la alineación de LLM pero dañan la precisión: Arranque del enrutamiento de personas basado en intención con PRISM
es.summary: Las personas expertas en LLM mejoran la alineación y la seguridad pero pueden reducir la precisión en tareas generales; la brecha depende del tipo de modelo y el diseño de la solicitud. Los investigadores desarrollaron PRISM, un framework que utiliza enrutamiento de personas basado en intención que mantiene los beneficios de alineación sin sacrificar el rendimiento de tareas discriminativas.

title: Correlation-Weighted Multi-Reward Optimization for Compositional Generation
summary: Text-to-image models struggle with multi-concept prompts, often omitting or partially satisfying requested attributes. A new correlation-weighted optimization framework balances competing concepts by adaptively weighting rewards, achieving state-of-the-art results on compositional generation benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.18528v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Text-to-image models produce images that align well with natural language prompts, but compositional generation has long been a central challenge. Models often struggle to satisfy multiple concepts within a single prompt, frequently omitting some concepts and resulting in partial success. Such failures highlight the difficulty of jointly optimizing multiple concepts during reward optimization, where competing concepts can interfere with one another. To address this limitation, we propose Correlation-Weighted Multi-Reward Optimization (\ours), a framework that leverages the correlation structure among concept rewards to adaptively weight each attribute concept in optimization. By accounting for interactions among concepts, \ours balances competing reward signals and emphasizes concepts that are partially satisfied yet inconsistently generated across samples, improving compositional generation. Specifically, we decompose multi-concept prompts into pre-defined concept groups (\eg, objects, attributes, and relations) and obtain reward signals from dedicated reward models for each concept. We then adaptively reweight these rewards, assigning higher weights to conflicting or hard-to-satisfy concepts using correlation-based difficulty estimation. By focusing optimization on the most challenging concepts within each group, \ours encourages the model to consistently satisfy all requested attributes simultaneously. We apply our approach to train state-of-the-art diffusion models, SD3.5 and FLUX.1-dev, and demonstrate consistent improvements on challenging multi-concept benchmarks, including ConceptMix, GenEval 2, and T2I-CompBench.

zh.title: 相关加权多奖励优化用于组成生成
zh.summary: 文本到图像模型在处理多概念提示时遇到困难，通常会省略或部分满足所请求的属性。新的相关加权优化框架通过自适应加权奖励来平衡竞争概念，在组成生成基准上达到了最先进的结果。

fr.title: Optimisation multi-récompense pondérée par corrélation pour la génération compositionnelle
fr.summary: Les modèles texte-à-image ont du mal avec les invites multi-concepts, omettant souvent ou satisfaisant partiellement les attributs demandés. Un nouveau cadre d'optimisation pondéré par corrélation équilibre les concepts concurrents en pondérant adaptatativement les récompenses, obtenant des résultats à la pointe de la technologie sur les repères de génération compositionnelle.

de.title: Korrelationsgewichtete Multi-Reward-Optimierung für zusammengesetzte Generierung
de.summary: Text-zu-Bild-Modelle haben Schwierigkeiten mit Multi-Konzept-Prompts und lassen oft angeforderte Attribute weg oder erfüllen sie nur teilweise. Ein neues korrelationsgewichtetes Optimierungs-Framework balanciert konkurrierende Konzepte durch adaptive Gewichtung von Belohnungen und erzielt modernste Ergebnisse bei Benchmarks für zusammengesetzte Generierung.

es.title: Optimización de múltiples recompensas ponderada por correlación para generación composicional
es.summary: Los modelos de texto a imagen luchan con indicaciones multi-concepto, a menudo omitiendo o satisfaciendo parcialmente los atributos solicitados. Un nuevo marco de optimización ponderada por correlación equilibra conceptos competidores ponderando adaptativamente las recompensas, logrando resultados de última generación en puntos de referencia de generación composicional.

title: Reasonably reasoning AI agents can avoid game-theoretic failures in zero-shot, provably
summary: Reasoning-capable AI agents can achieve Nash-like strategic equilibrium without explicit post-training when deployed in repeated interactions. Research proves agents naturally exhibit reasoning patterns that converge to stable equilibrium behaviors, potentially obviating the need for universal alignment procedures.
content:
arXiv:2603.18563v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI agents are increasingly deployed in interactive economic environments characterized by repeated AI-AI interactions. Despite AI agents' advanced capabilities, empirical studies reveal that such interactions often fail to stably induce a strategic equilibrium, such as a Nash equilibrium. Post-training methods have been proposed to induce a strategic equilibrium; however, it remains impractical to uniformly apply an alignment method across diverse, independently developed AI models in strategic settings. In this paper, we provide theoretical and empirical evidence that off-the-shelf reasoning AI agents can achieve Nash-like play zero-shot, without explicit post-training. Specifically, we prove that `reasonably reasoning' agents, i.e., agents capable of forming beliefs about others' strategies from previous observation and learning to best respond to these beliefs, eventually behave along almost every realized play path in a way that is weakly close to a Nash equilibrium of the continuation game. In addition, we relax the common-knowledge payoff assumption by allowing stage payoffs to be unknown and by having each agent observe only its own privately realized stochastic payoffs, and we show that we can still achieve the same on-path Nash convergence guarantee. We then empirically validate the proposed theories by simulating five game scenarios, ranging from a repeated prisoner's dilemma game to stylized repeated marketing promotion games. Our findings suggest that AI agents naturally exhibit such reasoning patterns and therefore attain stable equilibrium behaviors intrinsically, obviating the need for universal alignment procedures in many real-world strategic interactions.

zh.title: 理性推理的AI代理可以零样本地避免博弈论失败，可证明地
zh.summary: 具有推理能力的AI代理在重复交互中部署时可以无需明确后训练就能实现类纳什均衡。研究证明代理自然展现推理模式收敛到稳定均衡行为，可能无需通用对齐程序。

fr.title: Les agents IA capable de raisonnement peuvent éviter les défaillances théoriques des jeux en zero-shot, de manière prouvable
fr.summary: Les agents IA capables de raisonnement peuvent atteindre un équilibre stratégique de type Nash sans entraînement post-explicite lorsqu'ils sont déployés dans des interactions répétées. La recherche prouve que les agents affichent naturellement des schémas de raisonnement qui convergent vers des comportements d'équilibre stable, éliminant potentiellement le besoin de procédures d'alignement universelles.

de.title: Vernünftig denkende KI-Agenten können spieltheoretische Fehler im Zero-Shot-Modus nachweislich vermeiden
de.summary: KI-Agenten mit Denkfähigkeit können Nash-ähnliches strategisches Gleichgewicht ohne explizites Post-Training bei wiederholten Interaktionen erreichen. Forschung zeigt, dass Agenten natürlicherweise Denkmuster aufweisen, die zu stabilem Gleichgewichtsverhalten konvergieren, was möglicherweise universelle Ausrichtungsverfahren überflüssig macht.

es.title: Los agentes de IA que razonan razonablemente pueden evitar fallos teóricos de juegos en cero-shot, de manera comprobable
es.summary: Los agentes de IA capaces de razonar pueden lograr un equilibrio estratégico similar al de Nash sin entrenamiento posterior explícito cuando se despliegan en interacciones repetidas. La investigación demuestra que los agentes exhiben naturalmente patrones de razonamiento que convergen a comportamientos de equilibrio estable, lo que potencialmente elimina la necesidad de procedimientos de alineación universal.

title: CAPSUL: A Comprehensive Human Protein Benchmark for Subcellular Localization
summary: CAPSUL is a new benchmark combining 3D protein structures with expert-annotated subcellular localization data, enabling structure-based models to improve drug target identification. The dataset demonstrates that structural features are crucial for accurate localization prediction in clinical applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.18571v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Subcellular localization is a crucial biological task for drug target identification and function annotation. Although it has been biologically realized that subcellular localization is closely associated with protein structure, no existing dataset offers comprehensive 3D structural information with detailed subcellular localization annotations, thus severely hindering the application of promising structure-based models on this task. To address this gap, we introduce a new benchmark called $\mathbf{CAPSUL}$, a $\mathbf{C}$omprehensive hum$\mathbf{A}$n $\mathbf{P}$rotein benchmark for $\mathbf{SU}$bcellular $\mathbf{L}$ocalization. It features a dataset that integrates diverse 3D structural representations with fine-grained subcellular localization annotations carefully curated by domain experts. We evaluate this benchmark using a variety of state-of-the-art sequence-based and structure-based models, showcasing the importance of involving structural features in this task. Furthermore, we explore reweighting and single-label classification strategies to facilitate future investigation on structure-based methods for this task. Lastly, we showcase the powerful interpretability of structure-based methods through a case study on the Golgi apparatus, where we discover a decisive localization pattern $\alpha$-helix from attention mechanisms, demonstrating the potential for bridging the gap with intuitive biological interpretability and paving the way for data-driven discoveries in cell biology.

zh.title: CAPSUL：亚细胞定位的全面人蛋白基准
zh.summary: CAPSUL是一个新的基准，结合了3D蛋白质结构和专家标注的亚细胞定位数据，使基于结构的模型能够改进药物靶标识别。该数据集证明了结构特征对于临床应用中准确的定位预测至关重要。

fr.title: CAPSUL : Un benchmark complet de protéines humaines pour la localisation subcellulaire
fr.summary: CAPSUL est un nouveau benchmark combinant des structures protéiques 3D avec des données de localisation subcellulaire annotées par des experts, permettant aux modèles basés sur la structure d'améliorer l'identification des cibles de médicaments. L'ensemble de données démontre que les caractéristiques structurelles sont cruciales pour une prédiction précise de la localisation dans les applications cliniques.

de.title: CAPSUL: Ein umfassendes Benchmark für subzelluläre Lokalisierung menschlicher Proteine
de.summary: CAPSUL ist ein neues Benchmark, das 3D-Proteinstrukturen mit von Experten kommentierten Daten zur subzellulären Lokalisierung kombiniert und es strukturbasierten Modellen ermöglicht, die Arzneimittelzielidentifizierung zu verbessern. Der Datensatz zeigt, dass strukturelle Merkmale für eine genaue Lokalisierungsvorhersage in klinischen Anwendungen entscheidend sind.

es.title: CAPSUL: Un benchmark integral de proteínas humanas para localización subcelular
es.summary: CAPSUL es un nuevo benchmark que combina estructuras de proteínas 3D con datos de localización subcelular anotados por expertos, permitiendo que los modelos basados en estructuras mejoren la identificación de objetivos de fármacos. El conjunto de datos demuestra que las características estructurales son cruciales para la predicción precisa de localización en aplicaciones clínicas.

title: Interplay: Training Independent Simulators for Reference-Free Conversational Recommendation
summary: A reference-free simulation framework trains independent LLMs as user and recommender agents that interact without predetermined target items, generating realistic conversational data at scale. The approach produces more diverse, authentic interactions than traditional single-model simulation methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.18573v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Training conversational recommender systems (CRS) requires extensive dialogue data, which is challenging to collect at scale. To address this, researchers have used simulated user-recommender conversations. Traditional simulation approaches often utilize a single large language model (LLM) that generates entire conversations with prior knowledge of the target items, leading to scripted and artificial dialogues. We propose a reference-free simulation framework that trains two independent LLMs, one as the user and one as the conversational recommender. These models interact in real-time without access to predetermined target items, but preference summaries and target attributes, enabling the recommender to genuinely infer user preferences through dialogue. This approach produces more realistic and diverse conversations that closely mirror authentic human-AI interactions. Our reference-free simulators match or exceed existing methods in quality, while offering a scalable solution for generating high-quality conversational recommendation data without constraining conversations to pre-defined target items. We conduct both quantitative and human evaluations to confirm the effectiveness of our reference-free approach.

zh.title: Interplay：为无参考对话推荐训练独立模拟器
zh.summary: 一个无参考模拟框架训练独立的大语言模型作为用户和推荐代理，它们在没有预定义目标项的情况下互动，大规模生成真实的对话数据。这种方法比传统的单模型模拟方法产生更多样化、更真实的交互。

fr.title: Interplay : Entraîner des simulateurs indépendants pour la recommandation conversationnelle sans référence
fr.summary: Un cadre de simulation sans référence entraîne des modèles linguistiques de grande taille indépendants en tant qu'agents utilisateurs et recommandeurs qui interagissent sans éléments cibles prédéterminés, générant des données conversationnelles réalistes à grande échelle. L'approche produit des interactions plus diversifiées et plus authentiques que les méthodes de simulation traditionnelles basées sur un seul modèle.

de.title: Interplay: Unabhängige Simulatoren für referenzfreie konversationelle Empfehlungen trainieren
de.summary: Ein referenzfreies Simulationsframework trainiert unabhängige große Sprachmodelle als Benutzer- und Empfehlungsagenten, die ohne vorgegebene Zielartikel interagieren und realistische Konversationsdaten in großem Maßstab generieren. Das Verfahren erzeugt vielfältigere und authentischere Interaktionen als herkömmliche Single-Model-Simulationsmethoden.

es.title: Interplay: Entrenar simuladores independientes para recomendación conversacional sin referencia
es.summary: Un marco de simulación sin referencia entrena modelos de lenguaje grande independientes como agentes de usuario y recomendadores que interactúan sin elementos de destino predeterminados, generando datos conversacionales realistas a escala. Este enfoque produce interacciones más diversas y auténticas que los métodos de simulación tradicionales de un solo modelo.

title: MedForge: Interpretable Medical Deepfake Detection via Forgery-aware Reasoning
summary: MedForge provides interpretable detection of AI-generated deepfakes in medical scans through expert-guided reasoning supervision and a 90K-sample benchmark. The system offers pre-hoc, evidence-grounded verdicts aligned with clinical expertise, addressing critical trust and safety concerns in healthcare AI.
content:
arXiv:2603.18577v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Text-guided image editors can now manipulate authentic medical scans with high fidelity, enabling lesion implantation/removal that threatens clinical trust and safety. Existing defenses are inadequate for healthcare. Medical detectors are largely black-box, while MLLM-based explainers are typically post-hoc, lack medical expertise, and may hallucinate evidence on ambiguous cases. We present MedForge, a data-and-method solution for pre-hoc, evidence-grounded medical forgery detection. We introduce MedForge-90K, a large-scale benchmark of realistic lesion edits across 19 pathologies with expert-guided reasoning supervision via doctor inspection guidelines and gold edit locations. Building on it, MedForge-Reasoner performs localize-then-analyze reasoning, predicting suspicious regions before producing a verdict, and is further aligned with Forgery-aware GSPO to strengthen grounding and reduce hallucinations. Experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art detection accuracy and trustworthy, expert-aligned explanations.

zh.title: MedForge：通过伪造感知推理进行可解释的医学深度伪造检测
zh.summary: MedForge通过专家引导的推理监督和90K样本基准，提供对医学扫描中AI生成的深度伪造的可解释检测。该系统提供与临床专业知识相一致的前期、证据基础的判决，解决了医疗保健AI中的关键信任和安全问题。

fr.title: MedForge : Détection Interprétable des Deepfakes Médicaux via un Raisonnement Conscient de la Falsification
fr.summary: MedForge fournit une détection interprétable des deepfakes générés par l'IA dans les images médicales grâce à une supervision du raisonnement guidée par des experts et un benchmark de 90K échantillons. Le système offre des verdicts ex-ante, fondés sur des preuves et alignés avec l'expertise clinique, répondant aux préoccupations critiques en matière de confiance et de sécurité dans l'IA médicale.

de.title: MedForge: Interpretable Medical Deepfake Detection via Forgery-aware Reasoning
de.summary: MedForge bietet eine interpretierbare Erkennung von KI-generierten Deepfakes in medizinischen Bildern durch expertengeleite Begründungsüberwachung und einen 90K-Sample-Benchmark. Das System bietet vorhersagende, evidenzgestützte Urteile, die mit klinischem Fachwissen übereinstimmen, und löst kritische Vertrauens- und Sicherheitsbedenken in der Gesundheits-KI.

es.title: MedForge: Detección Interpretable de Deepfakes Médicos mediante Razonamiento Consciente de Falsificación
es.summary: MedForge proporciona detección interpretable de deepfakes generados por IA en imágenes médicas a través de supervisión de razonamiento guiada por expertos y un conjunto de referencia de 90K muestras. El sistema ofrece veredictos pre-hoc, basados en evidencia y alineados con la experiencia clínica, abordando preocupaciones críticas de confianza y seguridad en la IA sanitaria.

title: ZEBRAARENA: A Diagnostic Simulation Environment for Studying Reasoning-Action Coupling in Tool-Augmented LLMs
summary: ZebraArena is a benchmark for evaluating reasoning-action coupling in tool-augmented LLMs through procedurally generated diagnostic tasks. Frontier models like GPT-5 achieve only 60% accuracy on hard instances and use 70-270% more tool calls than theoretically optimal, revealing persistent reasoning-action gaps.
content:
arXiv:2603.18614v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Tool-augmented large language models (LLMs) must tightly couple multi-step reasoning with external actions, yet existing benchmarks often confound this interplay with complex environment dynamics, memorized knowledge or dataset contamination. In this paper, we introduce ZebraArena, a procedurally generated diagnostic environment for studying reasoning-action coupling in tool-augmented LLMs, with controllable difficulty and a knowledge-minimal design, which limits gains from memorization or dataset contamination. Each task in ZebraArena requires a set of critical information which is available only through targeted tool use, yielding an interpretable interface between external information acquisition and deductive reasoning. This design provides deterministic evaluation via unique solutions, and a theoretical optimal query count for measuring efficient tool use. We show that ZebraArena requires a combination of in-depth reasoning and accurate external tool calling, which remains a challenge as frontier reasoning models such as GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 Pro only achieves 60% accuracy on the hard instances. We also observe a persistent gaps between theoretical optimality and practical tool usage. For example, GPT-5 uses 70-270% more tool calls than the theoretical optimum. We highlight the key findings in our evaluation, and hope ZebraArena stimulates further research on the interplay between internal reasoning and external action.

zh.title: ZEBRAARENA：用于研究工具增强LLM中推理-行动耦合的诊断模拟环境
zh.summary: ZebraArena是一个基准，用于通过程序生成的诊断任务评估工具增强LLM中的推理-行动耦合。前沿模型（如GPT-5）在困难实例上仅达到60%的准确率，并使用比理论最优值多70-270%的工具调用，暴露了持久的推理-行动差距。

fr.title: ZEBRAARENA : Un Environnement de Simulation Diagnostique pour Étudier le Couplage Raisonnement-Action dans les LLM Augmentés d'Outils
fr.summary: ZebraArena est un benchmark pour évaluer le couplage raisonnement-action dans les LLM augmentés d'outils grâce à des tâches diagnostiques générées de manière procédurale. Les modèles frontière comme GPT-5 ne réalisent que 60% de précision sur les instances difficiles et utilisent 70-270% plus d'appels d'outils que l'optimal théorique, révélant des lacunes persistantes raisonnement-action.

de.title: ZEBRAARENA: Eine diagnostische Simulationsumgebung zum Studium der Reasoning-Action-Kopplung in Tool-erweiterten LLMs
de.summary: ZebraArena ist ein Benchmark zur Bewertung der Reasoning-Action-Kopplung in Tool-erweiterten LLMs durch prozedural generierte diagnostische Aufgaben. Frontier-Modelle wie GPT-5 erreichen nur 60% Genauigkeit bei schwierigen Instanzen und verwenden 70-270% mehr Tool-Aufrufe als theoretisch optimal, was persistente Reasoning-Action-Lücken offenbart.

es.title: ZEBRAARENA: Un Entorno de Simulación Diagnóstica para Estudiar el Acoplamiento Razonamiento-Acción en LLMs Aumentados con Herramientas
es.summary: ZebraArena es un benchmark para evaluar el acoplamiento razonamiento-acción en LLMs aumentados con herramientas mediante tareas diagnósticas generadas procedimentalmente. Los modelos frontera como GPT-5 logran solo 60% de precisión en instancias difíciles y utilizan 70-270% más llamadas de herramientas que lo teóricamente óptimo, revelando brechas persistentes en razonamiento-acción.

title: Agentic Flow Steering and Parallel Rollout Search for Spatially Grounded Text-to-Image Generation
summary: AFS-Search is a training-free framework that uses a vision-language model as a semantic critic to dynamically steer text-to-image generation, enabling real-time spatial constraint feedback. The approach achieves state-of-the-art results on FLUX.1-dev with both high-performance and fast variants.
content:
arXiv:2603.18627v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Precise Text-to-Image (T2I) generation has achieved great success but is hindered by the limited relational reasoning of static text encoders and the error accumulation in open-loop sampling. Without real-time feedback, initial semantic ambiguities during the Ordinary Differential Equation trajectory inevitably escalate into stochastic deviations from spatial constraints. To bridge this gap, we introduce AFS-Search (Agentic Flow Steering and Parallel Rollout Search), a training-free closed-loop framework built upon FLUX.1-dev. AFS-Search incorporates a training-free closed-loop parallel rollout search and flow steering mechanism, which leverages a Vision-Language Model (VLM) as a semantic critic to diagnose intermediate latents and dynamically steer the velocity field via precise spatial grounding. Complementarily, we formulate T2I generation as a sequential decision-making process, exploring multiple trajectories through lookahead simulations and selecting the optimal path based on VLM-guided rewards. Further, we provide AFS-Search-Pro for higher performance and AFS-Search-Fast for quicker generation. Experimental results show that our AFS-Search-Pro greatly boosts the performance of the original FLUX.1-dev, achieving state-of-the-art results across three different benchmarks. Meanwhile, AFS-Search-Fast also significantly enhances performance while maintaining fast generation speed.

zh.title: 代理流程引导和并行展开搜索用于空间基础的文本到图像生成
zh.summary: AFS-Search是一个免训练框架，使用视觉语言模型作为语义评论员动态引导文本到图像生成，实现实时空间约束反馈。该方法在FLUX.1-dev上达到了最先进的结果，同时具有高性能和快速变体。

fr.title: Steering Agentic Flow et Parallel Rollout Search pour la Génération Texte-à-Image Spatialement Ancrée
fr.summary: AFS-Search est un cadre sans entraînement qui utilise un modèle vision-langage comme critique sémantique pour orienter dynamiquement la génération texte-à-image, permettant un retour en temps réel des contraintes spatiales. L'approche atteint des résultats de pointe sur FLUX.1-dev avec des variantes à la fois haute performance et rapides.

de.title: Agentic Flow Steering und Parallel Rollout Search für räumlich verankerte Text-zu-Bild-Generierung
de.summary: AFS-Search ist ein trainingsfreies Framework, das ein Vision-Language-Modell als semantischen Kritiker nutzt, um die Text-zu-Bild-Generierung dynamisch zu steuern und Echtzeit-Feedback zu räumlichen Einschränkungen zu ermöglichen. Der Ansatz erreicht modernste Ergebnisse auf FLUX.1-dev mit hochleistungs- und schnellen Varianten.

es.title: Steering de Flujo Agéntico y Búsqueda Paralela de Despliegue para Generación de Texto a Imagen Espacialmente Anclada
es.summary: AFS-Search es un marco sin entrenamiento que utiliza un modelo visión-lenguaje como crítico semántico para dirigir dinámicamente la generación de texto a imagen, habilitando retroalimentación de restricción espacial en tiempo real. El enfoque logra resultados de vanguardia en FLUX.1-dev con variantes tanto de alto rendimiento como rápidas.

title: D-Mem: A Dual-Process Memory System for LLM Agents
summary: D-Mem is a hybrid memory system combining fast vector retrieval with high-fidelity full deliberation for long-horizon LLM reasoning. A gating policy dynamically routes queries between processes, recovering 96.7% of full deliberation performance at significantly lower computational cost.
content:
arXiv:2603.18631v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Driven by the development of persistent, self-adapting autonomous agents, equipping these systems with high-fidelity memory access for long-horizon reasoning has emerged as a critical requirement. However, prevalent retrieval-based memory frameworks often follow an incremental processing paradigm that continuously extracts and updates conversational memories into vector databases, relying on semantic retrieval when queried. While this approach is fast, it inherently relies on lossy abstraction, frequently missing contextually critical information and struggling to resolve queries that rely on fine-grained contextual understanding. To address this, we introduce D-Mem, a dual-process memory system. It retains lightweight vector retrieval for routine queries while establishing an exhaustive Full Deliberation module as a high-fidelity fallback. To achieve cognitive economy without sacrificing accuracy, D-Mem employs a Multi-dimensional Quality Gating policy to dynamically bridge these two processes. Experiments on the LoCoMo and RealTalk benchmarks using GPT-4o-mini and Qwen3-235B-Instruct demonstrate the efficacy of our approach. Notably, our Multi-dimensional Quality Gating policy achieves an F1 score of 53.5 on LoCoMo with GPT-4o-mini. This outperforms our static retrieval baseline, Mem0$^\ast$ (51.2), and recovers 96.7\% of the Full Deliberation's performance (55.3), while incurring significantly lower computational costs.

zh.title: D-Mem：大型语言模型智能体的双过程记忆系统
zh.summary: D-Mem是一个混合记忆系统，将快速向量检索与高保真完整推敲相结合，用于长期LLM推理。一个门控策略在过程之间动态路由查询，以明显降低的计算成本恢复96.7%的完整推敲性能。

fr.title: D-Mem : Un système de mémoire à double processus pour les agents LLM
fr.summary: D-Mem est un système de mémoire hybride combinant la récupération vectorielle rapide avec une délibération complète haute fidélité pour le raisonnement LLM à long terme. Une politique de portail achemine dynamiquement les requêtes entre les processus, récupérant 96,7% des performances de délibération complète à un coût informatique nettement inférieur.

de.title: D-Mem: Ein Dual-Process-Speichersystem für LLM-Agenten
de.summary: D-Mem ist ein hybrides Speichersystem, das schnelle Vektorabrufung mit hochgradig treuer vollständiger Überlegung für LLM-Überlegungen über lange Horizonte verbindet. Eine Gate-Richtlinie leitet Abfragen dynamisch zwischen Prozessen weiter und stellt 96,7% der vollständigen Überlegungsleistung bei deutlich geringeren Rechenkosten wieder her.

es.title: D-Mem: Un sistema de memoria de proceso dual para agentes LLM
es.summary: D-Mem es un sistema de memoria híbrido que combina la recuperación rápida de vectores con deliberación completa de alta fidelidad para el razonamiento LLM de largo horizonte. Una política de compuerta enruta dinámicamente consultas entre procesos, recuperando el 96,7% del rendimiento de deliberación completa con un costo computacional significativamente menor.

title: An Onto-Relational-Sophic Framework for Governing Synthetic Minds
summary: The Onto-Relational-Sophic framework proposes governance approaches for AI systems beyond traditional tool-centric models, grounding digital personhood in philosophy and relational theory. It offers proportionate governance recommendations for autonomous research agents, AI-mediated healthcare, and agentic ecosystems.
content:
arXiv:2603.18633v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence, from task-specific systems to foundation models exhibiting broad, flexible competence across reasoning, creative synthesis, and social interaction, has outpaced the conceptual and governance frameworks designed to manage it. Current regulatory paradigms, anchored in a tool-centric worldview, address algorithmic bias and transparency but leave unanswered foundational questions about what increasingly capable synthetic minds are, how societies should relate to them, and the normative principles that should guide their development. Here we introduce the Onto-Relational-Sophic (ORS) framework, grounded in Cyberism philosophy, which offers integrated answers to these challenges through three pillars: (1) a Cyber-Physical-Social-Thinking (CPST) ontology that defines the mode of being for synthetic minds as irreducibly multi-dimensional rather than purely computational; (2) a graded spectrum of digital personhood providing a pragmatic relational taxonomy beyond binary person-or-tool classifications; and (3) Cybersophy, a wisdom-oriented axiology synthesizing virtue ethics, consequentialism, and relational approaches to guide governance. We apply the framework to emergent scenarios including autonomous research agents, AI-mediated healthcare, and agentic AI ecosystems, demonstrating its capacity to generate proportionate, adaptive governance recommendations. The ORS framework charts a path from narrow technical alignment toward comprehensive philosophical foundations for the synthetic minds already among us.

zh.title: 用于治理合成思维的本体-关系-智慧框架
zh.summary: 本体-关系-智慧框架提出了超越传统工具中心模型的AI系统治理方法，将数字人格基于哲学和关系理论。它为自主研究智能体、AI介导的医疗和智能体生态系统提供了相称的治理建议。

fr.title: Un cadre onto-relationnel-sophique pour gouverner les esprits synthétiques
fr.summary: Le cadre onto-relationnel-sophique propose des approches de gouvernance pour les systèmes d'IA au-delà des modèles traditionnels centrés sur les outils, ancrant la personnalité numérique dans la philosophie et la théorie relationnelle. Il offre des recommandations de gouvernance proportionnées pour les agents de recherche autonomes, les soins de santé assistés par l'IA et les écosystèmes d'agents.

de.title: Ein onto-relationales-sophisches Rahmenwerk zur Steuerung synthetischer Geister
de.summary: Das onto-relationale-sophische Rahmenwerk schlägt Governance-Ansätze für KI-Systeme jenseits traditioneller werkzeugzentrierter Modelle vor und verankert digitale Persönlichkeit in Philosophie und Relationstheorie. Es bietet angemessene Governance-Empfehlungen für autonome Forschungsagenten, KI-vermittelte Gesundheitswesen und agentengestützte Ökosysteme.

es.title: Un marco onto-relacional-sofístico para gobernar mentes sintéticas
es.summary: El marco onto-relacional-sofístico propone enfoques de gobernanza para sistemas de IA más allá de los modelos tradicionales centrados en herramientas, fundamentando la personalidad digital en la filosofía y la teoría relacional. Ofrece recomendaciones de gobernanza proporcionadas para agentes de investigación autónomos, atención médica mediada por IA y ecosistemas de agentes.

title: Balanced Thinking: Improving Chain of Thought Training in Vision Language Models
summary: SCALe improves vision-language model training by separately weighting reasoning traces and answers during supervised fine-tuning, avoiding verbose reasoning and inaccurate answers. The method achieves comparable performance to full two-phase SFT+RL pipelines while requiring only one-seventh of training time.
content:
arXiv:2603.18656v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multimodal reasoning in vision-language models (VLMs) typically relies on a two-stage process: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL). In standard SFT, all tokens contribute equally to the loss, even though reasoning data are inherently token-imbalanced. Long  traces overshadow short but task-critical  segments, leading to verbose reasoning and inaccurate answers. We propose SCALe (Scheduled Curriculum Adaptive Loss), which explicitly separates supervision over reasoning and answer segments using dynamic, length-independent weighting. Unlike vanilla SFT, which overweights the  segment, SCALe-SFT gradually shifts the focus from  to  throughout training via a cosine scheduling policy, encouraging concise and well-grounded reasoning. We evaluate SCALe across diverse benchmarks and architectures. Results show that SCALe consistently improves accuracy over vanilla SFT and matches the performance of the full two-phase SFT + GRPO pipeline while requiring only about one-seventh of the training time, making it a lightweight yet effective alternative. When combined with GRPO, SCALe achieves the best overall performance, highlighting its value both as a standalone method and as a strong foundation for reinforcement refinement.

zh.title: 平衡思维：改进视觉语言模型中的思维链训练
zh.summary: SCALe通过在监督微调期间分别加权推理轨迹和答案来改进视觉语言模型训练，避免冗长的推理和不准确的答案。该方法实现了与完整的两阶段SFT+RL管道相当的性能，但仅需要七分之一的训练时间。

fr.title: Pensée équilibrée : Amélioration de la formation en chaîne de pensée dans les modèles de vision-langage
fr.summary: SCALe améliore l'entraînement des modèles de vision-langage en pondérant séparément les traces de raisonnement et les réponses lors du fine-tuning supervisé, en évitant le raisonnement verbeux et les réponses inexactes. La méthode atteint des performances comparables aux pipelines SFT+RL complets à deux phases tout en ne nécessitant qu'un septième du temps d'entraînement.

de.title: Ausgewogenes Denken: Verbesserung des Chain-of-Thought-Trainings in Vision-Language-Modellen
de.summary: SCALe verbessert das Training von Vision-Language-Modellen durch separate Gewichtung von Überlegungsspuren und Antworten während des betreuten Fine-Tunings und vermeidet ausführliches Denken und ungenaue Antworten. Die Methode erreicht vergleichbare Leistung mit vollständigen zweiphasigen SFT+RL-Pipelines, während nur ein Siebtel der Trainingszeit erforderlich ist.

es.title: Pensamiento equilibrado: Mejora del entrenamiento de cadena de pensamiento en modelos de visión-lenguaje
es.summary: SCALe mejora el entrenamiento del modelo visión-lenguaje al ponderar por separado los rastros de razonamiento y las respuestas durante el ajuste fino supervisado, evitando razonamientos verbosos y respuestas inexactas. El método logra un rendimiento comparable a las canalizaciones SFT+RL de dos fases completas mientras requiere solo una séptima parte del tiempo de entrenamiento.

title: Thinking with Constructions: A Benchmark and Policy Optimization for Visual-Text Interleaved Geometric Reasoning
summary: GeoAux-Bench introduces 4,334 geometry problems with visual-textual construction steps, revealing that interleaved multimodal aids outperform static diagrams for reasoning. A reinforcement learning approach trains models to strategically construct visual aids, improving performance through selective auxiliary representations.
content:
arXiv:2603.18662v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Geometric reasoning inherently requires "thinking with constructions" -- the dynamic manipulation of visual aids to bridge the gap between problem conditions and solutions. However, existing Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) are largely confined to passive inference with static diagrams, lacking the strategic knowledge of when and how to construct effective visual aids. To address this, we present a framework for Visual-Text Interleaved Chain-of-Thought. We first introduce GeoAux-Bench, the first benchmark comprising 4,334 geometry problems that aligns textual construction steps with ground-truth visual updates. Our pilot study reveals two critical insights: (1) interleaved visual-textual aids outperform single-modality counterparts, which cannot losslessly capture geometric synergy; and (2) valid constructions act as entropy reducers, strongly correlating with reduced reasoning perplexity. Building on these findings, we propose Action Applicability Policy Optimization (A2PO), a reinforcement learning paradigm for mastering strategic construction. A2PO employs Adaptive Reward Shaping to regulate the timing and quality of visual aids via counterfactual sampling to distinguish necessary from redundant constructions. Experiments demonstrate our approach enables MLLMs to leverage selective auxiliary constructions, yielding a 3.51% gain over strong baselines. Code and data are available on GitHub.

zh.title: 用构造思考：视觉-文本交错几何推理的基准和策略优化
zh.summary: GeoAux-Bench引入了4,334个几何问题，具有视觉-文本构造步骤，表明交错的多模态辅助比静态图表对推理更有效。强化学习方法训练模型战略性地构造视觉辅助，通过选择性辅助表示来改进性能。

fr.title: Penser avec les constructions : un benchmark et optimisation politique pour le raisonnement géométrique entrelacé visual-texte
fr.summary: GeoAux-Bench introduit 4 334 problèmes de géométrie avec des étapes de construction visual-texte, révélant que les aides multimodales entrelacées surpassent les diagrammes statiques pour le raisonnement. Une approche d'apprentissage par renforcement entraîne les modèles à construire stratégiquement des aides visuelles, améliorant les performances par des représentations auxiliaires sélectives.

de.title: Denken mit Konstruktionen: Ein Benchmark und Richtlinienoptimierung für visuell-textliche verflochtene geometrische Argumentation
de.summary: GeoAux-Bench führt 4.334 Geometrieprobleme mit visuell-textlichen Konstruktionsschritten ein und zeigt, dass verflochtene multimodale Hilfsmittel statische Diagramme für das Schließen übertreffen. Ein Verstärkungslernansatz trainiert Modelle, um strategisch visuelle Hilfsmittel zu konstruieren und die Leistung durch selektive Hilfsdarstellungen zu verbessern.

es.title: Pensar con construcciones: un punto de referencia y optimización de políticas para razonamiento geométrico entrelazado visual-texto
es.summary: GeoAux-Bench introduce 4.334 problemas de geometría con pasos de construcción visual-texto, revelando que los auxilios multimodales entrelazados superan a los diagramas estáticos para el razonamiento. Un enfoque de aprendizaje por refuerzo entrena modelos para construir estratégicamente ayudas visuales, mejorando el desempeño a través de representaciones auxiliares selectivas.

title: MANAR: Memory-augmented Attention with Navigational Abstract Conceptual Representation
summary: MANAR applies Global Workspace Theory to attention mechanisms, achieving linear-time complexity through an abstract conceptual representation workspace. The architecture matches or exceeds standard attention across language, vision, and speech tasks while enabling non-convex synthesis impossible with quadratic attention.
content:
arXiv:2603.18676v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: MANAR (Memory-augmented Attention with Navigational Abstract Conceptual Representation), contextualization layer generalizes standard multi-head attention (MHA) by instantiating the principles of Global Workspace Theory (GWT). While MHA enables unconstrained all-to-all communication, it lacks the functional bottleneck and global integration mechanisms hypothesized in cognitive models of consciousness. MANAR addresses this by implementing a central workspace through a trainable memory of abstract concepts and an Abstract Conceptual Representation (ACR). The architecture follows a two-stage logic that maps directly to GWT mechanics: (i) an integration phase, where retrieved memory concepts converge to form a collective "mental image" (the ACR) based on input stimuli; and (ii) a broadcasting phase, where this global state navigates and informs the contextualization of individual local tokens. We demonstrate that efficient linear-time scaling is a fundamental architectural byproduct of instantiating GWT functional bottleneck, as routing global information through a constant-sized ACR resolves the quadratic complexity inherent in standard attention. MANAR is a compatible re-parameterization of MHA with identical semantic roles for its projections, enabling knowledge transfer from pretrained transformers via weight-copy and thus overcoming the adoption barriers of structurally incompatible linear-time alternatives. MANAR enables non-convex contextualization, synthesizing representations that provably lie outside the convex hull of input tokens - a mathematical reflection of the creative synthesis described in GWT. Empirical evaluations confirm that MANAR matches or exceeds strong baselines across language (GLUE score of 85.1), vision (83.9% ImageNet-1K), and speech (2.7% WER on LibriSpeech), positioning it as an efficient and expressive alternative to quadratic attention.

zh.title: MANAR：带有导航抽象概念表示的记忆增强注意机制
zh.summary: MANAR将全局工作区理论应用于注意机制，通过抽象概念表示工作区实现线性时间复杂度。该架构在语言、视觉和语音任务中匹配或超越标准注意，同时实现用二次注意不可能的非凸合成。

fr.title: MANAR : Attention augmentée de mémoire avec représentation conceptuelle abstraite navigationnelle
fr.summary: MANAR applique la Global Workspace Theory aux mécanismes d'attention, réalisant une complexité linéaire via un espace de travail de représentation conceptuelle abstraite. L'architecture égale ou surpasse l'attention standard dans les tâches de langage, vision et parole tout en permettant la synthèse non-convexe impossible avec l'attention quadratique.

de.title: MANAR: Speicher-augmentierte Aufmerksamkeit mit navigativer abstrakter konzeptioneller Darstellung
de.summary: MANAR wendet die Global Workspace Theory auf Aufmerksamkeitsmechanismen an und erreicht lineare Zeitkomplexität durch einen abstrakten konzeptionellen Darstellungsarbeitsbereich. Die Architektur entspricht oder übertrifft die Standardaufmerksamkeit bei Sprach-, Sicht- und Sprachaufgaben und ermöglicht nicht-konvexe Synthesen, die bei quadratischer Aufmerksamkeit unmöglich sind.

es.title: MANAR: Atención aumentada con memoria con representación conceptual abstracta navegacional
es.summary: MANAR aplica la Global Workspace Theory a los mecanismos de atención, logrando una complejidad de tiempo lineal a través de un espacio de trabajo de representación conceptual abstracta. La arquitectura iguala o supera la atención estándar en tareas de lenguaje, visión y habla mientras permite síntesis no-convexa imposible con atención cuadrática.

title: Accurate and Efficient Multi-Channel Time Series Forecasting via Sparse Attention Mechanism
summary: Li-Net uses sparse Top-K attention and multimodal embeddings to capture linear and non-linear dependencies across channels in multi-channel time series forecasting. The architecture balances prediction accuracy with computational efficiency, delivering competitive performance with significantly lower memory usage.
content:
arXiv:2603.18712v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The task of multi-channel time series forecasting is ubiquitous in numerous fields such as finance, supply chain management, and energy planning. It is critical to effectively capture complex dynamic dependencies within and between channels for accurate predictions. However, traditional method paid few attentions on learning the interaction among channels. This paper proposes Linear-Network (Li-Net), a novel architecture designed for multi-channel time series forecasting that captures the linear and non-linear dependencies among channels. Li-Net dynamically compresses representations across sequence and channel dimensions, processes the information through a configurable non-linear module and subsequently reconstructs the forecasts. Moreover, Li-Net integrates a sparse Top-K Softmax attention mechanism within a multi-scale projection framework to address these challenges. A core innovation is its ability to seamlessly incorporate and fuse multi-modal embeddings, guiding the sparse attention process to focus on the most informative time steps and feature channels. Through the experiment results on multiple real-world benchmark datasets demonstrate that Li-Net achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art baseline methods. Furthermore, Li-Net provides a superior balance between prediction accuracy and computational burden, exhibiting significantly lower memory usage and faster inference times. Detailed ablation studies and parameter sensitivity analyses validate the effectiveness of each key component in our proposed architecture.
  Keywords: Multivariate Time Series Forecasting, Sparse Attention Mechanism, Multimodal Information Fusion, Non-linear relationship

zh.title: 通过稀疏注意机制进行精确高效的多通道时间序列预测
zh.summary: Li-Net使用稀疏Top-K注意和多模态嵌入来捕捉多通道时间序列预测中通道间的线性和非线性依赖关系。该架构平衡了预测准确性和计算效率，以显著降低内存使用的方式提供竞争性性能。

fr.title: Prévision de séries chronologiques multi-canal précise et efficace via mécanisme d'attention clairsemé
fr.summary: Li-Net utilise l'attention sparse Top-K et les plongements multimodaux pour capturer les dépendances linéaires et non-linéaires entre les canaux dans la prévision de séries chronologiques multi-canal. L'architecture équilibre la précision de la prédiction avec l'efficacité informatique, offrant des performances compétitives avec une utilisation de mémoire considérablement réduite.

de.title: Genaue und effiziente Multi-Kanal-Zeitreihenprognose über einen spärlichen Aufmerksamkeitsmechanismus
de.summary: Li-Net verwendet spärliche Top-K-Aufmerksamkeit und multimodale Einbettungen, um lineare und nicht-lineare Abhängigkeiten zwischen Kanälen bei der Multi-Kanal-Zeitreihenprognose zu erfassen. Die Architektur balanciert Vorhersagegenauigkeit mit Recheneffizienz und liefert wettbewerbsfähige Leistung bei deutlich geringerem Speicherverbrauch.

es.title: Pronóstico de series de tiempo multicanal preciso y eficiente a través de mecanismo de atención disperso
es.summary: Li-Net utiliza atención sparse Top-K e incrustaciones multimodales para capturar dependencias lineales y no-lineales entre canales en pronóstico de series de tiempo multicanal. La arquitectura equilibra la precisión de predicción con eficiencia computacional, ofreciendo desempeño competitivo con un uso de memoria significativamente reducido.
